issue_title: config verifier trips up on DEFAULT configs inherited by deprecated options
issue_body:
Apply the following patch diff, which represents moving the checkstyle task from the compile goal to the lint goal, at a6d90633c7:

```
diff --git a/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py b/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
index 106aa0763..828af8137 100644
--- a/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
+++ b/pants-plugins/src/python/internal_backend/optional/register.py
@@ -11,5 +11,5 @@ from pants.goal.task_registrar import TaskRegistrar as task
 
 
 def register_goals():
-  task(name='checkstyle', action=Checkstyle).install('compile')
+  task(name='checkstyle', action=Checkstyle).install('lint')
   task(name='scalastyle', action=Scalastyle).install('compile')
diff --git a/pants.ini b/pants.ini
index 0f4cab06f..f7e9f0a44 100644
--- a/pants.ini
+++ b/pants.ini
@@ -129,7 +129,7 @@ deps: ["3rdparty/python:thrift"]
 antlr3_deps: ["3rdparty/python:antlr-3.1.3"]
 
 
-[compile.checkstyle]
+[lint.checkstyle]
 configuration: %(pants_supportdir)s/checkstyle/coding_style.xml
 
 [compile.errorprone]
diff --git a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
index 872eab91b..041a32c5a 100644
--- a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
+++ b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py
@@ -23,6 +23,8 @@ class Checkstyle(NailgunTask):
 
   :API: public
   """
+  deprecated_options_scope = 'compile.checkstyle'
+  deprecated_options_scope_removal_version = '9.9.9.dev0'
 
   _CHECKSTYLE_MAIN = 'com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.Main'
```

Now:

```
gonzo:[~/src/pants][master]$ ./pants
WARN] /Users/benjyw/src/pants/src/python/pants/option/options_bootstrapper.py:171: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: scope compile.checkstyle will be removed in version 9.9.9.dev0.
  Use scope lint.checkstyle instead (options: jvm_options)
  valid_options_under_scope = set(options.for_scope(scope))

No goals specified.
Use `pants goals` to list goals.
Use `pants help` to get help.
```

Whereas:

```
gonzo:[~/src/pants][master]$ ./pants --no-verify-config
No goals specified.
Use `pants goals` to list goals.
Use `pants help` to get help.
```

What's happening is that we have `jvm_options` in the `DEFAULT` section of our pants.ini, and the verifier sees this inherited into the deprecated scope. But obviously this doesn't represent a true use of deprecated options, so we shouldn't display this message in this case.

issue_title: `clilib` allow custom names for sub-commands passed to ArgumentParser
issue_body: Right now, subcommand names for `clilib.ArgumentParser` can only be function names. Thus, reserved words like `class` are impossible and names like `list` would cause shadowing of built-ins where the function is defined. So there should be a way to register callables as commands under custom names.

issue_title: Multiproc files not cleaned up if worker gets segfault/oom/...
issue_body:
In multiproc mode, if one of the gunicorn workers gets killed because of a segfault or out-of-memory situation, it doesn't get a chance to clean up its `.db` files. These files are thus left in the multiproc folder, distorting the metrics.

Maybe this can be solved by moving the cleanup hook into the gunicorn "master" process. My suggestion is to solve this in the Python client instead, by deleting all `.db` files with dead PIDs before aggregating the metrics.

issue_title: removing criteria breaks if shared set is empty + solutions
issue_body:
For googleads-python-lib/examples/adwords/v201502/advanced_operations/find_and_remove_criteria_from_shared_set.py  
The code provided currently breaks if it encounters a negative shared set of keywords that is empty, with no keywords or placements.  
This may or may not be a deliberate design decision to crash the program if it encounters a shared set that has no keywords or placements.
But for people who are using this code for more than just finding and removing criteria and/or people who wouldn't like their code to stop as soon as it encounters an empty shared sets, I have some fixes/workarounds for this behavior (that don't involve try). 

There are two reasons that this code throws errors and breaks when it encounters an empty shared set.  For each of the reasons, I have provided, solutions and/or workarounds to these problems, the probable cause of the program-stopping errors, as well as the error output.

Exhibit A: 
Problem:
When the code sets the initial values to look for criteria (line108), it sets totalNumEntries to be 1 in page.  This gets it into get into the loop looking at the result of page = shared_criterion_service.get(selector) (line 111) and then tries to look at page['entries'], where it then crashes, because page doesn't have 'entries' because the shared set it is looking at has no entries.  
error:
Printed below is the page(with a print page statement) for an empty shared set and the error that the code throws:
(SharedCriterionPage){
   totalNumEntries = 0
   Page.Type = "SharedCriterionPage"
 }
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 423, in <module>
    newly_visited_shared_sets = main(adwords_client, campaign[0], visited_shared_sets, shared_set_table)
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 164, in main
    if page['entries']:
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/sudsobject.py", line 154, in __getitem__
    return getattr(self, name)
AttributeError: 'SharedCriterionPage' object has no attribute 'entries'
Solution:  
One solution (that I've implemented) is to check that the totalNumEntries is still more than 0 before trying to access page['entries'] in the line if page['entries']:
      if page['totalNumEntries'] > offset:
            if page['entries']: 
Another potential solution that I haven't tested is, instead of writing 
      if page['entries']:
at line 112 instead write 
      if 'entries' in page:
as written in line 79 of this code, since that page query doesn't break if a campaign doesn't have shared sets so this syntax seems to work.

Exhibit B:
Problem:
The code breaks at  response = shared_criterion_service.mutate(operations) (line 145)
if the operations doesn't have any criterion_ids to work on, aka if criterion_ids is an empty list.   I don't know why asking it to remove nothing breaks it, but I do have a solution. 
Error:
Printed below is the page for an empty shared set and the error given when trying to run this program
(SharedCriterionPage){
   totalNumEntries = 0
   Page.Type = "SharedCriterionPage"
 }
2015-06-23 13:40:08     suds.client ERROR    <suds.sax.document.Document instance at 0x1232ab8>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 423, in <module>
    newly_visited_shared_sets = main(adwords_client, campaign[0], visited_shared_sets, shared_set_table)
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 205, in main
    response = shared_criterion_service.mutate(operations)
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/googleads/common.py", line 296, in MakeSoapRequest
    for arg in args])
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/client.py", line 521, in __call__
    return client.invoke(args, kwargs)
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/client.py", line 581, in invoke
    result = self.send(soapenv)
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/client.py", line 619, in send
    description=tostr(e), original_soapenv=original_soapenv)
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/client.py", line 670, in process_reply
    raise WebFault(fault, replyroot)
suds.WebFault: Server raised fault: '[NotEmptyError.EMPTY_LIST @ operations]'
Solution:  
You do not need to do the remove operation, or request a response for it if there are no criterion_ids to remove
Therefore, I placed the block of code responsible for removing criterion and reporting on it in an if clause in order to check if there are any criterion_ids to remove before trying to remove any criteria
    if criterion_ids: 
        block of code for removing and reporting on criterions (lines134-151)


I hoped that helped you!

issue_title: Feedback: Improve error message when `charm build` not a charm layer not found
issue_body:
## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client
 - [X] Provide versions of tools used
 - [X] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `snap info charm` and got the following ouput:

```
name:      charm
summary:   charm and charm-tools
publisher: charms
contact:   juju@lists.ubuntu.com
license:   unknown
description: |
  charmstore-client and charm-tools
commands:
  - charm
snap-id:      2Rryoc2ylScfbFl4eQtpntHD9iuZuMvt
tracking:     stable
refresh-date: 2018-01-17
channels:                                          
  stable:    2.2.3                     (114) 103MB -
  candidate: 2.2.3                     (114) 103MB -
  beta:      ↑                                     
  edge:      2.2.4-dev+git-18-gefbc5db (150) 41MB  -
installed:   2.2.3                     (114) 103MB -
```

I am using: Ubuntu 18.04

## Issue/Feature

Feedback: Improve error message when `charm build` not a charm layer not found

### I expect/expected the following

If I run `charm build` from a directory that is not a charm layer, the error message I receive should be more opinionated about why it failed. We can detect when we're missing `metadata.yaml` and `layer.yaml`, and assume we're not in a layer folder.

```bash
$ cd ~/charms/layers
$ charm create my-new-charm
$ charm build
build: Destination charm directory: ~/charms/layers
build: The top level layer expects a valid layer.yaml file
build: At least one layer must provide metadata.yaml
build: The current directory doesn't appear to be a valid charm layer.
```


### What I got

The charm builds successfully but will not deploy:
```bash
$ cd ~/charms/layers
$ charm create my-new-charm
$ charm build
build: Destination charm directory: ~/charms/layers
build: Please add a `repo` key to your layer.yaml, with a url from which your layer can be cloned.
build: The top level layer expects a valid layer.yaml file
build: Processing layer: layers (from .)
build: At least one layer must provide metadata.yaml
```

issue_title: Watchdog routes not announced
NpipeSocket.settimeout - Blocking and non-blocking are mixed up
issue_body: `settimeout(None)` puts the socket in non-blocking mode, `settimeout(0)` in blocking mode. Specification indicates it should be the other way around.

issue_title: Simultaneous detach and remove not supported
issue_body:
Hi,

Detach (-d) and remove(--rm) options are not simultaneously supported by the python API but no longer conflict as of docker version 1.13.0. [See here in the code.](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/models/containers.py#L679)

There was a pull request that moves the --rm option daemon-side and allows for running containers detached with the removal option: https://github.com/docker/docker/pull/20848

Do you think you can remove that API restriction?

Cheers

issue_title: Incorrect MAP inference result for VariableElimination and BeliefPropagation on 2-site Markov Model
issue_body:
I have a simple code where the inference on a 2-site Markov Net seems to be incorrect:
There are two binary sites a and b with one factor:

mm = MarkovModel([("a", "b")])
factor_ab = DiscreteFactor(variables=["a", "b"], cardinality=[2, 2], values=[0, 3, 2, 2])

bp = BeliefPropagation(model=mm)
bp.calibrate()
ve = VariableElimination(model=mm)

If I ask for the most likely value of a or b, it should yield "1" in both cases (in absence of evidence), but neither VE nor BP give the correct answer:

>>> ve.map_query(variables=["a"])
{'a': 0}
>>> ve.map_query(variables=["b"])
{'b': 1}

And BP gives the same result. However, both correctly predict the joined most probable assignment:
>>> ve.map_query(variables=["a", "b"])
{"a": 0, "b": 1}

issue_title: Keep Glyphs custom parameter does not work
issue_body:
After doing some testing on files that use the `Keep Glyphs` custom parameter (Noto Sans Devanagari, Noto Sans Bengali, Noto Sans Kannada, Noto Sans Malayalam) it was clear that nothing at all was happening. I see a couple problems so far.

font_project.py
```
276    def save_otfs(self,
277                  ufos,
278                  ttf=False,
279                  is_instance=False,
280                  interpolatable=False,
281                  use_afdko=False,
282                  autohint=None,
283                  subset=None,
   ...
406        for font, ufo in zip(fonts, ufos):
407            if subset is None:
408                export_key = GLYPHS_PREFIX + 'Glyphs.Export'
409                subset = ((GLYPHS_PREFIX + 'Keep Glyphs') in ufo.lib or
410                          any(glyph.lib.get(export_key, True) is False
411                              for glyph in ufo))
412            if subset:
413                self.subset_otf_from_ufo(otf_path, ufo)
   ...
446        keep_glyphs_list = ufo.lib.get(GLYPHS_PREFIX + 'Keep Glyphs')
```
subset is initialized to None so the first time through the for loop it is None, but after that it gets set to either True or False and any subsequent iteration doesn't necessarily get to the subset code. Each ufo needs to be checked for the presence of Keep Glyphs because it could be different for each one. In Noto Sans Bengali all glyphs are set to export so `any(glyph.lib.get(export_key, True) is False for glyph in ufo)` is False and so is `(GLYPHS_PREFIX + 'Keep Glyphs') in ufo.lib` because GLYPHS_PREFIX + 'Keep Glyphs' is never set in the ufo in the first place. 

I adjusted things locally so that everything works, but when it does work we run into another big problem: https://github.com/googlei18n/glyphsLib/issues/205
If Keep Glyphs works and removes the encoded glyphs from the UI instances (because those are replaced with UI specific glyphs) then those codepoints need to be assigned to the UI specific glyphs. Otherwise they just show up as .notdef. That's way worse than the current behavior which is to completely miss the Keep Glyphs which leaves the non-UI glyphs in the UI instances.

issue_title: Figure out how to get spacy to tokenize wiki text correctly
issue_body:
SQuAD has plenty of paragraphs that have wiki notes, formatting like "This was a protest.[note 4]".  Spacy for some reason does not tokenize these strings correctly, giving "protest.[note" as a single token.  We should be able to improve performance on SQuAD at least a little bit by fixing these issues, as it affects a fair number of our training examples, and some of the dev set.

A test that currently fails, but should pass (goes in [`word_splitter_test.py`](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/tests/data/tokenizers/word_splitter_test.py)):

```python
 def test_tokenize_handles_wiki_notes(self):
     passage = "McWhorter writes of Lee, \"for a white person from the South to write a " +\
             "book like this in the late 1950s is really unusual\u2014by its very existence " +\
             "an act of protest.\"[note 4] Author James McBride calls Lee brilliant but " +\
             "stops short of calling her brave: \"I think by calling Harper Lee brave you " +\
             "kind of absolve yourself of your own racism.\""
     tokens, offsets = self.word_splitter.split_words(passage)
     assert "protest" in tokens
```

issue_title: ec2: Consider using EBS volume for etcd
issue_body:
When running a 5 node cluster, stopping and starting a node causes the cluster to fail.

Using the cloud formation scripts do the following:

* Boot a 5 node cluster in amazon.
* Ensure etcd is running and has consensus.
* Stop a node (thus wiping out ephemeral storage).
* Start the Node.

Expected: Cluster Unaffected, Node Rejoin
Actual: Cluster-wide failure, Ephemeral disk fails to mount, All nodes crash etcd.

Solution?

Don't use ephemeral storage for etcd.

issue_title: Add a helper for Task Wait
issue_body:
We started talking about the potential merit of having the SDK handle Task Wait itself.

I think the simplest approach is to have a method that times out and returns True/False depending on whether or not the task completed.

Basic signature and behavior I'd like to recommend:
```python
def task_wait(task_id, timeout=10, polling_interval=1):
    """
    Wait until a Task is complete or fails, with a time limit. If the task is "ACTIVE" after time
    runs out, returns ``False``. Otherwise returns ``True``.

    :param uuid task_id: ID of the Task to wait for completion
    :param int timeout: Number of seconds to wait in total. Minimum 1
    :param int polling_interval: Number of seconds between queries to Globus about the
    Task status. Minimum 1
    """
    ...
```

I think it's important to support both a timeout and a polling interval, to let clients make their own decisions about how long to wait and what to do between polling periods.
By way of example, I could see a system working multiple transfers with lax latency requirements wanting to cycle between them in a single thread.


There's a viable approach, for supporting the CLI heartbeats, of adding a generic "polling callback" that gets the results of `get_task(task_id)` every time it's called and is assumed to take 0 seconds.
Consider:

```python
def task_wait(task_id, timeout=10, polling_interval=1, polling_callback=None):
    """
    ...

    :param uuid task_id: ID of the Task to wait for completion
    ...
    :param callable polling_callback: A function which takes a Task document, as
    returned by ``TransferClient.get_task``, as its only argument. Called after each
    Task status check to perform an arbitrary action between checks.

    Note: we assume that ``polling_callback`` takes 0 seconds to run.

    For example, one could use the ``polling_callback`` to print a simple message
    between checks.

    >>> def update_ticker(task_doc):
    >>>     print('.', end='')
    >>> client.task_wait(tid, polling_callback=update_ticker)

    This will print a dot every second, until the task completes or 10 seconds run out.
    """
    ...
```

That example replaces the following messy segment:

```python
>>> retries = 10
>>> counter = 0
>>> done_stat = False
>>> while counter < retries and not done_stat:
...     print('.', end='')
...     done_stat = client.task_wait(tid, timeout=1)
...
......
>>> done_stat
True
```

To me, that ability to inject functionality between the polling calls simplifies things tremendously.
So long as we state that we assume it to take 0 seconds, it doesn't add any code complexity internally either.

issue_title: Convert library to use a registry pattern for encoders and decoders.
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

The way `eth-abi` is structured, it cannot be extended without changes to the core library.  This is problematic because new ABI types are likely to be created (as seen [here](http://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/abi-spec.html#handling-tuple-types)).

### How can it be fixed?

We'll create a new `ABIRegistry` class with the following functionality.

```
registry = ABIRegistry()

# register an encoder and a decoder for a static type.
registry.register("string", string_encoder, string_decoder)

# register only an encoder or decoder
registry.register_encoder("string", string_encoder)
registry.register_decoder("string", string_decoder)

# register a dynamic encoder/decoder
registry.register(lambda base, sub, arrlist: bool(arrlist), array_encoder, array_decoder)

# register an exact static type.
registry.register(("uint", "256", "[]", string_encoder, string_decoder)

# the main encode/decode API
encode_abi = registry.encode_abi
decode_abi = registry.decode_abi
encode_single = registry.encode_single
decode_single = registry.decode_single
```

Then, within the codebase, we make a default registry to expose all of the built-in encoders and decoders.

#### Implementation details.

**validation**

The `register` method should raise an exception if the same static type is registered twice.

**determining which encoder/decoder to use**

Internally, the `ABIRegistry` will need to implement a dynamic version of the `eth_abi.encoding.get_single_encoder` and `eth_abi.decoding.get_single_decoder`.  The logic for these functions should be as follows.

1. check for an exact match against static abi types.
2. evaluate all callbacks.  If exactly one (1) returns true, use that type.  Otherwise raise an appropriate exception.

issue_title: Keccak256 no longer picklable
issue_body: https://github.com/ethereum/eth-hash/commit/99d647e9832f12672c52556dbb21b4c38a75c35d makes `Keccak256` keep a reference to the backend module (e.g. `eth_hash.backends.pycryptodome`) whereas before it'd keep references to the backend's functions (`keccak256` and `preimage`). That is a problem because it means `Keccak256` is no longer picklable, which means we can't use it with, say, a `concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor`

issue_title: The anaconda metapackage should not pull from other channels
issue_body:
See this issue here: https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1778

This is fixed in master already, however, it was caused by the following confluence of factors:
1. The user has additional channels enabled besides the default.
2. Those channels contain packages with the same name as found in the main channel and included in the anaconda metapackage.
3. Those packages have additional dependencies not present in anaconda.
4. Those dependencies are not added to the search list, because anaconda links to *exact builds* (strictness=3), and the current tagged releases of conda do not add lower-level dependencies in such cases. This is fixed in master, but has been purposefully left unfixed because it degraded performance.

The anaconda metapackage ostensibly points to a set of curated and tested metapackages. My contention, therefore, is that anaconda should *only* refer to the exact packages supplied in the main channel, not identically named versions of packages found in other channels.

issue_title: activate and powerline don't play nice together
issue_body:
I am using [powerline](https://github.com/powerline/powerline) to display the current anaconda environment in my shell. However, when I use  `source active MY_ENV ` it doesn't realize that I am already using `powerline` to display the anaconda environment and it appends the current anaconda environment before my `PS1` variable. Effectively it now displays my environment twice in my shell.

One trivial workaround for this is to check whether `PS1` is modified by `powerline` before appending the current environment to it. I am happy to file a pull request, but I wanted to check how people feel about this and also if anybody else has any better ideas of how to deal with this.

issue_title: RFE: Interpolating on key level
issue_body:
Given that ini file:
```
[DEFAULT]
foo = 42

[s]
bar = 43
baz = %(foo)s
```

ConfigParser is able to return a value for key `foo` in section `s`:
```
print c.get('s', 'bar')  # 43
print c.get('s', 'baz')  # 42
print c.get('s', 'foo')  # 42
```

Using `ConfigObj` however, a `KeyError` is thrown:
```
print c['s']['bar']  # 43
print c['s']['baz']  # 42
print c['s']['foo']  # KeyError 'foo'
```

`ConfigParser`'s behavior is quite handy in some situations, as it allows to 'inherit' keys from a default section.

Any chance this could be implemented?

issue_title: Iterating over pileups is very inefficient
issue_body:
It seems that iterating over a pileup creates a PileupRead and an AlignedSegment object for each individual base in each column.  The entire sequence and qual strings are fetched, cached and then discarded for each read, and the CIGAR string is fetched and iterated over at lease 4 times (once to get the segment start and again to get the end for calling getSequenceInRange, and then again for getQualitiesInRange).  Some caching at the level of the IteratorColumn would be very beneficial here.

I'm curious if there has already been ideas around how to improve this situation.

issue_title: get_aligned_pairs with (matches_only=True, with_seq=True) in the presence of a deletion
issue_body:
Hi,

We've been trying to use the get_aligned_pairs(matches_only=True, with_seq=True) to parse the MD tag and identify the mismatched bases and categorise them by their mutation type.

I think we have identified a problem with the parsing code in the presence a deletion. Consider the following read:

    SRR1927066.11290428	99	chr1	14492	255	26M2D75M	=	14557	166	CCCATGGAGCACAGGCAGACAAAAGTCCGCCCCAGCTGTGTGGCCTCAAGCCAGCCTTCCGCTCCTTGAAGCTGGTCTCCACACAGTGCTGGTTCCGTCAC	CCCFFFFFHHHHHFGIJHIIJJJJFEHIGGHJJHGGFBGIHHEGGAFGGCC;=@A;CA>@BEBDDC;;;AC>A:9<:@C@CCCDDA:AC@:@?CCCBC<<@	MD:Z:21G4^CC75	PG:Z:MarkDuplicates	XG:i:2	NH:i:1	NM:i:3	XM:i:1	XN:i:0	XO:i:1	AS:i:-17	YS:i:0	YT:Z:CP

There is one mismatches in this read, but also there is a deletion. If we pull out the sequence of the genome and the read, the start looks like this:

    read:   CCCATGGAGCACAGGCAGACAAAAGT--CCGCCCCAGCTGTGTGGCCTCAAGCCAG
    genome: CCCATGGAGCACAGGCAGACAGAAGTCCCCGCCCCAGCTGTGTGGCCTCAAGCCAGC

Thus, the aligned pairs should not contain entries for the two deleted bases. But if we look at the output of get_aligned_pairs:

    [(0, 14491, 'C'),
     (1, 14492, 'C'),
     (2, 14493, 'C'),
     (3, 14494, 'A'),
     (4, 14495, 'T'),
     (5, 14496, 'G'),
     (6, 14497, 'G'),
     (7, 14498, 'A'),
     (8, 14499, 'G'),
     (9, 14500, 'C'),
     (10, 14501, 'A'),
     (11, 14502, 'C'),
     (12, 14503, 'A'),
     (13, 14504, 'G'),
     (14, 14505, 'G'),
     (15, 14506, 'C'),
     (16, 14507, 'A'),
     (17, 14508, 'G'),
     (18, 14509, 'A'),
     (19, 14510, 'C'),
     (20, 14511, 'A'),
     (21, 14512, 'g'),
     (22, 14513, 'A'),
     (23, 14514, 'A'),
     (24, 14515, 'G'),
     (25, 14516, 'T'),
     (26, 14519, 'C'), <----
     (27, 14520, 'C'), <----
     (28, 14521, 'C'),
     (29, 14522, 'C'),
     (30, 14523, 'G'),
     (31, 14524, 'C'),
     (32, 14525, 'C'),
     (33, 14526, 'C'),
     (34, 14527, 'C'),
     (35, 14528, 'A'),
     (36, 14529, 'G'),
     (37, 14530, 'C'),
     (38, 14531, 'T'), ...]

Note at the two highlighted bases, the genome indexes skip up two to represent the deletion, but bases in the third column don't. Thus after this point, none of the bases at the genome positions in the second column match that in the third column. e.g. the base at position 14521 is 'G' not 'C' as suggested get_aligned_pairs. 

We can get round this by doing:

    alignments = read.get_aligned_pairs(with_seq=True)
    alignments = [base for base in alignments
                          if base[0] is not None and base[1] is not None]

But its definitely a gotcha for people in the future.

issue_title: custom css for all of admin
issue_body:
Hi,
I have been trying to customise the CSS for the admin site with varying success.

I have implemented custom AdminForms, using the Media class (as it happens actually overriding the media property for my purposes, but the Media class worked as well) to communicate my custom CSS to the forms. That works fine.

I also use a custom AdminSite and, likewise, define a Media class with my custom CSS. To this custom AdminSite, I register all my forms, plus a few models from the standard site (User, Group). However the non customised admin forms (User and Group) as well as the supporting pages, (index, applications, logout etc) ignore this custom CSS.

The solution I found was twofold. First in the file django_admin_bootstrapped/templates/admin/base.html I think there should be a `{{ media.css }}` inserted in the "extrastyle" block. This propagates quite nicely since most of the derived templates call `{{ block.super }}`. Second in the function `each_context` inside sites.py, one needs to add a key for the "media" attribute to the dict that is returned. I did not really want to patch Django, so for my purpose I just wrote an override function for this in my custom AdminSite.

With these two changes in place it works better. I am happy with overriding the `each_context` function. Is it sensible to put the media.css patch in to this project?

Cheers,
Philip

Actually just checking has shown that, login and confirm action (at least) are still broken even with my changes above... (more to check)

issue_title: axis tuser
issue_body:
Hi

I tried to add TUSER to the axi stream VC. I'm currently stuck with the optional character of the signal.
How would you implement optional parameters in both stream_master_pkg and axi_stream_pkg?

The stream_master_pkg does not know the length of tuser, so having a push_stream function without it fails because it cannot push the correct vector to the message. Or should I modify the protocol if there is no tuser data (i.e. somethimes push 3 values, sometimes 2)?
So I just left stream_master_pkg as is (tuser is axi specific anyway) and made the axi_stream_master drive tuser=zero if the message was not axi specific.

However, on the slave side, this distinction does not work as there is no pop_axi_stream_msg. There is no client function to receive the tuser signal (no pop_axi_stream).
What would be the best way to implement this?

Thanks and best regards,
emanuel

issue_title: Failed to verify all nodes have repository access from one box but not another.
issue_body:
I've run across a situation that I find a little strange. I set up some monitoring jobs to curate my indices by optimizing, snapshotting and deleting older indices. I've been in the process of upgrading from 1.2.2 to 3.2.3 and testing from my local box everything is great and works as expected. But when I try to throw a job on my monitoring box I'm getting an error I find rather strange:

`2015-08-18 14:17:04,660 ERROR       curator.api.snapshot        create_snapshot:64   Failed to verify all nodes have repository access.`

At essentially the same time I run the same script from my local box and I have created an error for myself because I'm trying to test logstash picking up errors from curator snapshots and so what I expect to see is this:

`2015-08-18 14:15:10,184 INFO      Snapshot name: mdp-2015.08.09
2015-08-18 14:15:12,903 ERROR     A snapshot with name 'mdp-2015.08.09' already exists.
2015-08-18 14:15:12,903 WARNING   Job did not complete successfully.`

but instead I'm getting that first error where it fails to verify all nodes have repository access. I know that all nodes have repository access and my cluster is currently healthy. It seems strange that it works for my box and not on my monitoring server.

issue_title: Apparent inconsistency in requests and elasticsearch urllib3 dependency
issue_body:
In the latest setup.py commit (https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/commit/f1267b52f0deb2695317ffa54183b01c80c66801) the dependency on urllib3 was changed to 'urllib3<1.22,>=1.21.1'. The commit was titled "Fix urllib3 version to be compatible with requests". However, earlier that same day there had been a commit on requests (which is now in the latest release) to update the urllib3 dependency from 'urllib3>=1.21.1,<1.22' to 'urllib3>=1.21.1,<1.23' (https://github.com/requests/requests/commit/8d1228cd1943bfe89ffaae32e15b2feb3f8479f5).

So the question is, since requests 2.18.2-2.18.4 is ok with urllib3 1.22, is elasticsearch ok with urllib3 1.22?

issue_title: Scroll with scroll_id parameter is broken on ES 6.x
issue_body:
Continuing a scroll and clearing a scroll is broken against the latest master build of Elasticsearch.

The following snippet demonstrates the problems:

```python
import elasticsearch

# force content type header for ES 6.x
class ConfigurableHttpConnection(elasticsearch.Urllib3HttpConnection):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super(ConfigurableHttpConnection, self).__init__(**kwargs)
        self.headers.update({"Content-Type": "application/json"})


es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch(hosts=[{"host": "localhost", "port": 9200}], connection_class=ConfigurableHttpConnection)

# first page
r = es.search(body={
    "query": {
        "match_all": {}
    }
}, scroll="10s")

scroll_id=r["_scroll_id"]

try:
	# continue scrolling - will fail with HTTP 400
	es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
finally:
	# will fail too with HTTP 400
	es.clear_scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

Workaround: Explicitly define the request body, i.e instead of:

```python
es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

 we can use

```python
es.scroll(body={"scroll_id": self.scroll_id, "scroll": "10s"})
```

and instead of

```python
es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

we can use

```python
es.clear_scroll(body={"scroll_id": [scroll_id]})
```

Elasticsearch Python client version: 5.1.0

issue_title: Bulk Index timeout
issue_body:
passing `params=dict(request_timeout=100)` as
```
.bulk(..., params=dict(request_timeout=100))
```
works but when doing chunks it doesn't
```
.bulk(..., chunk_size=100, max_chunk_bytes=100000, params=dict(request_timeout=100))
```
because `params` pass to `elasticsearch.helpers.streaming_bulk` as `**kwargs` are then passed all the way down to `elasticsearch.transport.Transport#perform_request` and `kwargs` gets mutated by `timeout = params.pop('request_timeout', None)` which results in subsequents requests to be missing some parameters.

issue_title: "az network dns zone import" fails with error 'list index out of range'
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
I exported multiple BIND files from an existing Linux server to import them in Azure DNS. I am trying to import the zones using Azure CLI 2.0. According to [this document](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/dns-import-export) BIND files should be supported.
When executing the command "az network dns zone import -f "_bind-file_" -n "_zone-name_" -g _resourcegroup-name_", the command fails with the following error: 

> **list index out of range** 
>![afbeelding](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17471063/41534792-1d4773f2-7300-11e8-834b-610545b58de6.png)


**To Reproduce**

1. download the file attached.
[wpv.be.zone.txt](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/files/2111462/wpv.be.zone.txt)

1. run the command `az network dns zone import -f 'wpv.be.zone.txt' -n 'wpv.be' -g net-DNS` (_, where 'net-Dns' is the name of our current resourcegroup_)

issue_title: Issues with 'az network dns zone import'
issue_body:

issue_title: `ad sp create-for-rbac` should handle creating the role assignment
issue_body:
As dicussed here: https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/631#issuecomment-238400643

It would be helpful if the `create-for-rbac` command would also create the RBAC Role Assignments, including handling `ocp-aad-session-key` between the Graph/ARM calls so that we can painless create an SP and give it permissions all in one command.

If this isn't done, everyone who wants to create an SP from a script and give it permissions has to write logic to handle the Graph consistency issues.

issue_title: [App Service] Allow creating a service plan as part of web app creation
issue_body:
Being able to create a new web app as part of an existing plan makes a ton of sense, but I can't help but feel like it would simplify the getting started experience for users if the `az appservice web create` command would allow you to auto-create the plan for you, instead of needing to explicitly run `az appservice plan create` before hand. This would mirror the experience in the portal a little more closely, and optimize for single app plans.

I really like how the `az acs create` command chose to auto-generate a new service principal for you if you don't specify one, and I feel like this is another opportunity to streamline the experience, while still providing sufficient flexibility when/if needed.

issue_title: Uploading 'empty' blocks to page blobs is wasteful.
issue_body:
Re: `PageBlobService#create_blob_from_stream`

When a page blob is provisioned, the content is a stream of 0s. Therefore, when uploading a page blob, any block that is all zeros does not need to be uploaded, it can simply be skipped.

Most fixed-length VHD images are _mostly_ empty blocks, so uploading all those zeros represents a huge waste of time and bandwidth.

Our optimum solution for dealing with this in [azurectl](https://github.com/SUSE/azurectl/blob/master/azurectl/storage/page_blob.py) was to load a block from `/dev/zero` and compare it to the current block in the stream. If they are equal... move along to the next block without uploading.

See https://github.com/SUSE/azurectl/blob/master/azurectl/storage/page_blob.py#L50 for example.

And, just for reference, a 30GB openSUSE Leap 42.3 image, contains < 300MB of full blocks. Uploading with the API convenience methods, or `az`, results in an upload of 29.7GB of zeros. o.0

issue_title: Remove configuration items from __init__
issue_body:
*Problem*

Currently Client `__init__` method are:
```python
def __init__(
            self, credentials, subscription_id, api_version='2016-01-01', accept_language='en-US', long_running_operation_retry_timeout=30, generate_client_request_id=True, base_url=None, filepath=None):
```

We already found an issue with `long_running_operation_retry_timeout` being inconsistent. We want to remove all configuration parameters from the `__init__` to simplify the signature and be more open to new parameters in the future without re-generate the client. This reduce the file size too.

*Fix*

New signature will be:
```python
def __init__(self, credentials, subscription_id, api_version='2016-01-01'):
```

parameters are still available in the config attribute.
This code:
```python
client = Client(credentials, subscription_id, long_running_operation_retry_timeout=666)
```
becomes
```python
client = Client(credentials, subscription_id)
client.config.long_running_operation_timeout = 666
```

Feature equivalent, but we pushed advanced configuration to be used as an attribute and not a `__init__` method. This allows future code parameters to also works out of the box with no regeneration, since the config attribute is manage by msrestazure directly.

Since this is a breaking change for users using advanced configuration settings, planned for next major release of msrestaure

FYI @derekbekoe @johanste @annatisch @brettcannon

issue_title: question: global/indivisible hooks?
issue_body:
Many pre-commit hooks are appropriate to run per file -- flake8 and autopep8 are great examples.

Others, for example mypy and cyclic import detection, need to be run on an entire repository to work. In these cases

* Changes to one file can affect other files
* splitting a check into independent subchecks with xargs is inappropriate

Any thoughts on how to work with these in pre-commit? Pre-commit features that are helpful when working with these hooks include:
* environment management
* common configuration / configuration centralization
* include/exclude configuration

Thanks!

issue_title: Feature request: pre-commit or pre-push only hooks
issue_body:
I am writing a pre-commit hook that validates the author and committer emails of commits. However, these values can only be guaranteed to queried properly *after* the commit is created.

git does not provide utilities to retrieve this information in the pre-commit stage nor can you retrieve explicit information from the --author flag. (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/9627198/stop-a-git-commit-by-a-specific-author-using-pre-commit-hook)

I would like to ensure that this hook is only run at pre-push time. I can think of two implementations:

* yaml config file can specify pre-push or pre-commit only
* pre-push/pre-commit stage is made available via environmental variable when hook is run.

issue_title: Allow certain hook to be verbose
issue_body:
Hi,
While integrating flake8 as a hook, I'm trying to have certain checks (like complexity warnings) run on every commit but I don't necessarily want them to be blocking. flake8 has this very nice --exit-zero flag that allows this but the only way to get the output printed through pre-commit is to run pre-commit with --verbose but this prints a ton of useless information from other hooks.

My suggestion is to allow a 'verbose' flag to be set per hook as opposed to existing --verbose which is set globally. IMO it would allow to have extremely valuable information out of flake8 and pylint for instance. I could even run part of flake8 as being blocking, and other part as being non-blocking.
Let me know your thoughts, I'd be happy to work on this

issue_title: Hooks should have a way of specifying minimum pre-commit version to run
issue_body:
Obviously this is a bit of a chicken-and-egg problem until the feature exists, but afterwards this'll be useful.

For instance, the feature added in 0.6.6 (additional dependencies) would be useful to gate against older pre-commit versions where installation would be nonsensical.

This would be a fatal error that would require the user to upgrade pre-commit to use that hook.

I'd like to implement this first, and then change all of the mirror repositories to use the additional-deps approach.  This would alleviate the pains in #282 as well by eliminating the hacky mirroring technique that is currently required for node hooks.

issue_title: Raycast vehicle shaking when resting
issue_body:
In supertuxkart we are using  raycast vehicles from bullet, and I noticed that karts would shake when standing still. Karts would have a linear velocity of around +-0.2 to +-0.3 m/s forward/backward in different frames when no force was applied and the kart were basically at a standstill.

This seems to be caused by the friction handling of the raycast vehicle: calcRollingFriction would return just positive or just negative values.
While I didn't study the physics behind that part of the code, I guessed that because of the comment:
```
        // calculate j that moves us to zero relative velocity
```
it does calculate one impulse to be applied  to slow down the kart's rolling - but is ignoring the fact that there are actually 4 (or whatever) wheels - resulting in a 4-times too large impulse to be applied, causing the kart to reverse direction. So I divided the j1 value returned by calcRollingFriction by the number of wheels, and since then the shaking is basically gone. 
I can't say if this is indeed a bug in the physics, or if I was just lucky that the changed parameter worked in our case :) But I think it's worth having a look.

issue_title: List assignment extension reading invalid JSON
issue_body:
A problem I came across just now was to do with the list assignment extension.  This was in 0.4.0rc1

For the extension, we have `assignments = json.loads(stdout.decode())`, which has the effect of including any other printed lines in `nbgrader_config.py`.  I had an old debug line `print("I'm in the config file"` in my file, which led to JSON being invalid.

A possibility is to search for a regex string for JSON, e.g., `json.loads(re.findall("\[[^\]]*\]", stdout)[0])`

Possibly a better solution is to include a warning in the error message, because of course a regex is fallible as well!

issue_title: Logging and Clearing Output Slows Firefox
issue_body:
I noticed some performance issues in Firefox when I recently upgraded to the Jupyter notebook (4.0.5) from IPython Notebook (2.4.1). Firefox becomes really sluggish when running a cell that prints logging information to the output and is constantly being cleared by IPython.core.display.clear_output(). In the IPython Notebook (2.4.1) this logging method with clearing would work fine and not slowdown Firefox at all however in Jupyter (4.0.5) it gets really slow. An example might be:
```python
import logging
import sys
from IPython.core.display import clear_output

logger = logging.getLogger()
del logger.handlers[0]
logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
ch = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
ch.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
logger.addHandler(ch)

for num, i in enumerate(range(1000)):
    square = i**2
    clear_output(wait=True)
    logger.info("The square of element %d is %d", num, square)
```
I noticed this sluggish behavior because the logging and clearing function is used in a third party module I use for analysis however I created a notebook that gives me a similar result (attached same as above). I have tested this in Firefox and Firefox in safe mode. Google Chrome works fine.

http://nbviewer.ipython.org/urls/dl.dropbox.com/s/2gweawfwkd9ztg0/Logger_test.ipynb

issue_title: Caffe support
Error: TargetClassProbability criterion is inconsistent (PyTorch)
issue_body:
The `TargetClassProbability` criterion seems to behave stragely in some cases: for example, with `FashionMNIST`:

```
fmodel = foolbox.models.PyTorchModel(model, (0, 1), num_classes=10, channel_axis=, cuda=False)
criterion = foolbox.criteria.TargetClassProbability(target_class, 0.2)
attack = foolbox.attacks.ContrastReductionAttack(fmodel, criterion)
adv = torch.Tensor(attack2(np.array(img).reshape(1, 28, 28), img_class).view(1, 1, 28, 28)
```

gives an adversarial example with a classification probability of 83%

But then, in the exact same code, only raising the criteria target class probability to 0.5 causes the attack to fail (while it should theoretically work for any values under 0.83 at least).

Is this expected ?

issue_title: Underlying store max chunks limitation
issue_body:
The current openstack swift store has a limitation of 1000 chunks per file:
https://docs.openstack.org/swift/latest/overview_large_objects.html
> The number of object segments is limited to a configurable amount, default 1000. 
If we upload a file with more than this many chunks it is expected to fail.
With the current settings the current maximum file size is 100GB.

issue_title: scipy and hessian-vector-product
issue_body:
A very minor issue but thought I'd bring it up.

scipy.optimize.minimize expects hessian_vector_product(args, vector).

However, the hessian_vector_product in autograd returns a function like
hessian_vector_product(vector, args).

So for compatibility with scipy.optimize.minimize it would be more convenient to swap the order of the parameters.

But I am not so familiar with other optimizers in Python, and perhaps they expect the hessian_vector_product to be parametrized differently.

issue_title: Doc link broken
Support for skipping compiling the test project
issue_body:
In the current implementation, a small test project is always compiled using the selected generator, config and CMake option starting with `-DCMAKE_`.

When running test on continuous integration services like AppVeyor, configuring and compiling this small project seems to have an overhead of ~25s.

I suggest we introduce a new [scikit-build option](https://scikit-build.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#usage-scikit-build-options) named `--skip-generator-test` or we could simply skip the generator test if a used explicitly selected a generator.

issue_title: [Gaia] Additional driver hints
issue_body:
Update the storage logic to include the following:

* **timestamped tombstones**.  Be sure that the tombstone includes a UNIX timestamp, so duplicate deletes on the same data ID result in unique tombstones.

* **data pubkeys**.  Pass the public key or public key hash to the driver on `get_mutable_handler()` and `get_immutable_handler()`, in case the driver wants to fetch and verify a previously-stored tombstone.

issue_title: How to pull data points with signalflow in a (semi) non-blocking manner?
issue_body:
[The example code of executing a signalflow computation](https://github.com/signalfx/signalfx-python#executing-signalflow-computations) illustrates a way to retrieve data points where the data pulling is blocked at "computation.stream()" if non is reaady, quote

```python
program = "data('cpu.utilization').mean().publish()"
with signalfx.SignalFx().signalflow('MY_TOKEN') as flow:
    computation = flow.execute(program)
    for msg in computation.stream():  # <-- blocked here
        ...
```

Is there a way to "peek" at the stream to learn if there's data there, e.g., "computation.has_msg()"? Or make "computation.stream()" return if no msg is ready, instead of busy waiting in the inside while-loop?

And it is hanging forever after a few round tripped messages:

```python
import nsq
from tornado import ioloop

from tornado import gen
from uuid import uuid4
import time


class Writer(nsq.Writer):

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):

    def push(self, topic, message):
        future = gen.Future()

        def callback(conn, result):
            if result == 'OK':
                future.set_result(result)
            else:
                future.set_exception(result)

        self.pub(topic, message, callback=callback)
        return future

    def _on_connection_ready(self, conn, **kwargs):
        super(Writer, self)._on_connection_ready(conn, **kwargs)

        if not self._connected_future.done():
            self._connected_future.set_result(None)

    def wait_for_connection(self):
        return self._connected_future


class Reader(nsq.Reader):

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super(Reader, self).__init__(*args, message_handler=self.__handle_message, **kwargs)
        self._messages = Queue()

    def __handle_message(self, message):
        message.enable_async()
        self._messages.put_nowait(message)

    def pop(self):
        return self._messages.get()


@gen.coroutine
def main():
    channel = topic = 'test#ephemeral'
    nsqd_addresses = [
        'localhost:10100',
        'localhost:10200'
    ]
    reader = Reader(
        topic,
        channel,
        lookupd_http_addresses='http://localhost:10001',
        lookupd_poll_interval=1,
        max_in_flight=32,
        output_buffer_timeout=1
    )
    writer = Writer(nsqd_tcp_addresses=nsqd_addresses)
    yield writer.wait_for_connection()

    for i in xrange(10**6):
        message_text = uuid4().hex
        start_time = time.time()
        yield writer.push(topic, message_text)
        message = yield reader.pop()
        message.finish()
        assert message.body == message_text
        dt = time.time() - start_time
        print '[{}] Round-trip time: {:.02f}ms'.format(i, dt * 1000)


ioloop.IOLoop.current().run_sync(main)

```


The output sample:
```
[0] Round-trip time: 1189.58ms
[1] Round-trip time: 128.57ms
[2] Round-trip time: 0.48ms
[3] Round-trip time: 0.62ms
[4] Round-trip time: 0.95ms
# here it hangs on the 5th message
```

That's what I see in nsqadmin: 
![](http://i.imgur.com/We7wE5X.png)

Ready count values between two connections distributed not equally (32 vs 0). While in pynsq 0.8.0 it works fine (16 vs 16):

![](http://i.imgur.com/8QA0UKY.png)

I've found it useful to treat editor fields in the same way as author fields i.e. by writing a function that's the equivalent of `author()` that splits then into lists of 'Name, Surname`:

```
def editor(record):
    """
    Split editor field into a list of "Name, Surname".
    :param record: the record.
    :type record: dict
    :returns: dict -- the modified record.
    """
    if "editor" in record:
        if record["editor"]:
            record["editor"] = getnames([i.strip() for i in record["editor"].replace('\n', ' ').split(" and ")])
        else:
            del record["editor"]
    return record

Example:

>>> Host(id=1) == Host(id=1)
True

issue_title: Support Usergroups for entities.Host.owner field
issue_body:
Right now the `owner` field for Host entity is defined as
`'owner': entity_fields.OneToOneField(User),`
The issue here is `owner` depends on `owner_type` field, which can be one of 'User' or 'Usergroup'.
In case `owner_type == 'Usergroup`, the host can't be read/created, as nailgun will search for user, not for Usergroup.

issue_title: Tests stop with --exitfirst, even when rerun successfully
issue_body:
There currently is a problem with `--exitfirst`. In my understanding, the tests should stop when for the first time a test failed **after rerunning it n times**. This does happen, but in contrast the tests will also stop when a test failed and succeeded during a rerun.

So in other words, currently the test suite will be run normally and stops at first failure, but this failure will be run n times. However, I would expect the test suite to continue, when a failing test could be rerun successfully.

Part of the solution would be to reset the `--exitfirst` counter (internally it's implemented with `--maxfail num` with `num = 1` which stops after `num` failures).

issue_title: Make it more clear that users need to download the VS tooling installer for .NET Core in VS.
issue_body:
I have a dotnet core 1.1 project that I recently wanted to test the first run experience with the dotnet core tooling. (i.e. reformatting a machine for this purpose).

One thing that I found very confusing for the 1.1 installer is the following:

On the main page https://www.microsoft.com/net/core you believe you are downloading only dotnet core 1.0. Since I had upgraded to 1.1, I went directly to the other download page:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/core

This now gives me two options: `LTS` and `Current` (**Off-topic:** Why is LTS the default here and not current?)

Now since I've installed the bits quite a few times on different machines, I thought I could just install the Windows x64 SDK installer. However after installing only that and trying to load a project, I will get an incompatible framework error on loading my project.

`The imported project "C:\Program Files(x86)\MSBuild\Microsoft\VisualStudio\v14.0\DotNet\Microsoft.DotNet.Props" was not found.Confirm that the path in the <Import> declaration is correct, and that the file exist on disk.
`

This error doesn't really help me resolve the problem. It actually makes me go down the wrong path by searching on Stack Overflow/Forums/etc. However I then went back to the original page:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/core (Which has VS 2015 selected by default)

And it points to the correct tooling that I needed to install before installing the 1.1 runtime.

https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=827546

At this point I actually didn't know there was VS Tooling. I thought it was installed with the Windows SDK, but apparently you get 1.0 **AND** VS Tooling in this installer.

Because of this and myself skipping over the "Windows" guide to just get the 1.1 SDK, it wasn't obvious that I needed the VS tooling. However after installing the dotnet core VS tooling and then installing the 1.1 SDK, everything worked fine.

**Proposal:**

1) Add a note on the downloads page(https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/core) in which a pre-requisite to using dotnet core in Visual Studio is the dotnet core visual studio tooling.

2) Empower the error message above to let the user know that they might not have the dotnet core VS tooling installed even though they have dotnet core Windows SDK installed.

Introducing .NET IL Linker
issue_body:
# Introducing .NET IL Linker

The .NET Core team has been working on a linker in collaboration with the Mono team. It is based on the [Mono Linker](https://github.com/mono/linker) project, and by extension, on the [Cecil](https://github.com/jbevain/cecil) project.

The goal of the .NET IL Linker project is two part:

* Provide a capable linker for .NET Core to make it easy to significantly reduce .NET Core application sizes.
* Establish the Mono Linker as the primary linker for the .NET ecosystem.

You can start using the linker today, using the following instructions:

* [Using the .NET IL Linker](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/samples/linker-instructions.md)
* [Using IL Linker Advanced Features](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/samples/linker-instructions-advanced.md)

## Details

In trivial cases, the linker can reduce the size of applications by 50%. The size wins may be more favorable or more moderate for larger applications. The linker removes code in your application and dependent libraries that are not reached by any code paths. It is effectively an application-specific dead code analysis.

The IL Linker currently only supports [.NET Core Self Contained Applications](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/) with this first preview version. You can try using the linker with an example [self-contained .NET Core application sample](https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker-samples/blob/master/dotnetapp-selfcontained/README.md). The primary difference with this application is that it has a reference to the [ILLink.Tasks package](https://dotnet.myget.org/feed/dotnet-core/package/nuget/Illink.Tasks) in its [cspoj project file](https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet-docker-samples/blob/master/dotnetapp-selfcontained/dotnetapp.csproj).

The Linker provides an option to print a size improvement chart that describes binaries that have been removed from an application or that have been reduced in size. It also provides the total % size reduction for the application. You can see an example of one of these charts in the following example.

![.NET IL Linker Size Improvement Chart](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2608468/29877374-4d72ae62-8d54-11e7-9da3-3cc22f58f4f9.png)

Please provide feedback on your use of the linker. We are actively looking for feedback to improve the linker. In particular, we are looking for feedback on the following topics:

* Linker throughput.
* Cases where the linker can be more aggressive.
* Cases where the link is too aggressive and causes applications to fail at runtime.
* The linker provided an excellent result for a large application.

Please create issue with your feedback at either [mono/linker](https://github.com/mono/linker/issues) or [dotnet/core](https://github.com/dotnet/core/issues). Also feel free to contact the team at clrlinker@microsoft.com.

And, it wouldn't be right to announce this linker without a hat tip to [Joel Spolsky](https://www.joelonsoftware.com) who we can give the honorary and historical distinction of [feature requestor](https://www.joelonsoftware.com/2004/01/28/please-sir-may-i-have-a-linker/).

issue_title: Fix --csr by separating it from --no-lineages
issue_body: Currently, the `--csr` flag breaks under many circumstances because it implies a whole bunch of variations on how certs are stored.  None of that seems logically necessary.  `--csr` can work perfectly well with storage.py (it should probably imply `--domains <contents of CSR>`, etc) and we can have another flag (perhaps `--no-lineages` or `--dump-cert` or something) that causes the store-certs-in-the-current-directory-without-lineages behaviour.

issue_title: letsencrypt-auto install didn't remove SSLCertificateChainFile statement
issue_body:
I'm using Apache on Debian. I already had TLS configured, and thus had `SSLCertificateFile`, `SSLCertificateKeyFile` and `SSLCertificateChainFile` statements in my Apache site config.

letsencrypt-auto correctly replaced the values of the first two, but failed to change `SSLCertificateChainFile`, resulting in breakage.

Invocation:

```
./letsencrypt-auto install --apache -d example.com -d www.example.com --cert-path /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem --key-path /etc/letsencrypt/keys/0000_key-letsencrypt.pem --agree-dev-preview 
```

Site config before invocation:

```
<VirtualHost *:80>
        ServerName example.com
        ServerAlias www.example.com
        DocumentRoot ...

        ErrorLog ...
        CustomLog ...
</VirtualHost>

<VirtualHost *:443>
        ServerName example.com
        ServerAlias www.example.com
        DocumentRoot ...

        ErrorLog ...
        CustomLog ...

        SSLEngine on
        SSLCertificateFile ...
        SSLCertificateKeyFile ...
        SSLCertificateChainFile ...
</VirtualHost>
```

Apart from that, it worked nicely, and so we now finally have what CACert has kept promising for "next year" for the past 10 years or so. So thanks for all the work, letsencrypt is amazing! ❤

issue_title: Solution for permissions
issue_body:
make_or_verify_dir was initially written to avoid privilege escalation attacks.  It checks that the directory is owned by the current user.  Since files are overwritten and files are transferred into protected directories as root, it was important that those files were not edited by a lower privileged user.  Requiring blanket permissions was an easy way to avoid the vulnerabilities.  

Example vuln:  Backup configuration files for Apache will automatically be transferred into the configuration if Let's Encrypt fails... or rather if certain backup files exist.  Apache configuration files are Turing complete.

Now that the project has transitioned into a much more robust and feature heavy application.  This limitation can be confusing and annoying.

We need a finer-grained approach that maintains the protections where necessary, but also avoids unnecessary restrictions.

issue_title: Graceful shutdown with asyncio, internal tasks not cancelled
issue_body:
It looks like some of the tasks started by Telethon aren't being stopped when logging out.

Starting a bot up:

```python
self._client = TelegramClient(None, self._app_id, self._app_hash)
await self._client.start(bot_token=self._bot_token)
```

And shutting it down:

```python
await self._client.log_out()
self._client.disconnect()   
```

Gives me the following on exit:

```
ERROR:asyncio:Task was destroyed but it is pending!
task: <Task pending coro=<TelegramBareClient._recv_loop_impl() running at /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_bare_client.py:630> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[<TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x7ff0702814f8>()]>>
ERROR:asyncio:Task was destroyed but it is pending!
task: <Task pending coro=<TelegramBareClient._ping_loop_impl() running at /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_bare_client.py:593> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[<TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x7ff0702816d8>()]>>
ERROR:asyncio:Task was destroyed but it is pending!
task: <Task pending coro=<TelegramBareClient._state_loop_impl() running at /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/telethon/telegram_bare_client.py:598> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[<TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x7ff070281138>()]>>
```

These tasks are stored on the `TelegramBareClient` instance as `_recv_loop`, `_ping_loop` and `_state_loop` during startup, but aren't cancelled on disconnect.

Versions: Python 3.6.4, Telethon-aio 0.18.3

I'm using `django-pipeline` which compiles all the static assets into their versioned files, and then uses a json file to find those files when serving live content.

please either remove the file requirement from `ckeditor_uploader/templates/ckeditor/browse.html`, or put the file in place.

cheers,
László

issue_title: Powerline not working with bash - displaying ???'s for separators (powerline vim dispalys OK)
issue_body:
Currently the bash prompt showing as colour version, but with no glyphs, i.e.:

    ?username???/???opt???path1???path2???

`vim` is working as expected; the correct glyph for the hard separators.  

**The expected glyphs *are* visible if I `cat` the `powerline.json` files with the hard separator codes.**

Originally it wasn't displaying within vim, until I installed a patched font for for putty.  Even then it still wasn't visible on the shell prompt, however I at least go the *question marks to show* by setting the locale (see towards the end) and re-copying the main config:

    /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline/config_files/config.json 

to

     ~/.config/powerline/config.json

Calling powerline using: 

    POWERLINE_BASH_CONTINUATION=1 POWERLINE_BASH_SELECT=1 source /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/powerline/bindings/bash/powerline.sh

This is inside a docker instance using a centos7 image, with powerline installed using `pip` as per the setup instructions of the main powerline git repository. I did have to add the locale to the bare bones image:

    localedef -i en_GB -c -f UTF-8 en_GB.UTF-8

And I changed the locale to:

    LANG=en_GB.utf-8
    LC_CTYPE="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_NUMERIC="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_TIME="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_COLLATE="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_MONETARY="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_MESSAGES="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_PAPER="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_NAME="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_ADDRESS="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_TELEPHONE="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_MEASUREMENT="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_IDENTIFICATION="en_GB.utf-8"
    LC_ALL=en_GB.utf-8

I appreciate any help to try and fix this, and any tips on how to help diagnose the issue (even code edits) is appreciated.   

Is it possible for instance to print out the current theme and what configuration files are being used? 

It seems like this could be an issue with the render engine or perhaps an incompatibility with bash (*GNU bash, version 4.2.46(2)-release (x86_64-redhat-linux-gnu)*)?

**EDIT(1):** *Using `echo $PS1 | od -c -t x1` it looks as if the question marks are in the prompt itself.   An issue with the powerline render engine perhaps?*

issue_title: environment variables set as tmux segments never update value
issue_body:
I tried to set a couple of environment variables as tmux segments. Example:

```
{
  "function": "powerline.segments.common.env.environment",
    "args": {
      "variable": "tnumAll"
    }
 },
```

The variable does show up with the correct value in the correct location when I start a tmux session, but then it never changes even though the environment variable does change. I've confirmed this by typing `echo $tnumAll` and getting the correct value that differs from the one displayed in powerline bar in tmux. I have to close the session and start a new one to see the new values.

By contrast, setting this environment variable as a shell segment works as expected. With every new prompt line, the value displayed is the current one.

I tried looking around for similar reports but didn't find anything, so I'm not sure if this is expected behaviour, or if I'm missing some customization trick, or if it's a bug.

I'm seeing thing with:
powerline: 2.3
tmux: 2.1 (installed with Homebrew)
and iTerm 2.1.4 (latest stable) on OS X 10.11.1

issue_title: vmprof ideas - what is next?
issue_body:
There are several ideas, this issue summarizes them and encourages discussion:

- online profiling (or profile streaming)
- query runtime information. `python -m vmprof.stat -p <pid>` prints if profiling is enabled, how many bytes have been saved, ...
- network statistics (like statsd, profiles TCP and UDP timer and counters)

issue_title: Copy-on-write for states
issue_body:
## Description
Currently, when a new state gets created, all values from the previous state are deep-copied and mutated according to the instruction of the new state.
All the expressions that do not get changed by the mutation of the state do not have to be deep-copied.

### Benefits
Implementing this feature will save on used memory and speed, as deepcopy is an expensive operation.

child of #203

issue_title: IndexMetricsOut.bin source and regeneration
issue_body:
We receive data from a sequencing center that does not generate fastq data. Once we create a tag sheet here and generateFASTQ with our local copy of MiSeq Reporter the IndexmetricsOut.bin file is generated. If the file is incorrect e.g. the i7 is the wrong orientation, the SAV Indexing metrics are wrong and will not correct themselves when the SampleSheet is fixed and generateFASTQ is rerun. How can this file be updated to a new SampleSheet?

We also regularly rerun our MiSeq runs to generate Index files to check for tag errors, which 1) takes time to generate the files and 2) takes time to parse the index files, lots of time in the case of HiSeq runs. What files contain the data used to generate the IndexMetricsOut.bin file? I need to know the physical tag combinations, so I can't use the DemultiplexSummary files since it does not contain the pairing information.

issue_title: support vocab with indices assigned based on frequencies
issue_body:
Currently, the indices in `gluonnlp.Vocab` are assigned in the following order:
- assign indices to special tokens, including: \<unk\>, \<pad\>, \<bos\>, \<eos\> and reserved ones
- assign indices to normal tokens in the counter. 

What I see missing for the language model in https://arxiv.org/pdf/1602.02410.pdf is the capability to do the following. For large scale language models that require sampling during training, it's important to build a vocab with indices generated based on word frequencies (including \<unk\>, \<eos\>, etc):
- assign indices to special & normal tokens based their frequency. 
- If a normal token's frequency is less than `min_freq`, its frequency should contribute to the frequency of \<unk\>.  

I propose we extend `gluonnlp.data.Counter` and introduce `gluonnlp.SortedVocab`:
#### Counter
- \_\_init\_\_(iterable, unknown_token=None, min_freq=None)

unknown_token: if specified, tokens whose freq is under `min_freq` contribute to the frequency of provided unknown_token. 

min_freq: if specified, tokens whose freq is under `min_freq` will be removed from the counter.

#### SortedVocab

- \_\_init\_\_(counter=None, max_size=None, min_freq=1, unknown_token=C.UNK_TOKEN,
                 padding_token=C.PAD_TOKEN, bos_token=C.BOS_TOKEN, eos_token=C.EOS_TOKEN,
                 reserved_tokens=None)

Indexing and embedding attachment for text tokens. The indices in the vocabulary is generated based on frequencies of the tokens, including special tokens (unk, pad, eos, bos and reserved tokens). If max_size is specified, special tokens are still guaranteed to be in the vocabulary. The provided counter instance is the source of all tokens' frequencies.

@leezu @sxjscience @szha @cgraywang

issue_title: debug build shared lib is stripped with make install
issue_body:
Using `python setup.py install` is fine but `make install` is not. Using `readelf --debug-dump=decodedline /usr/lib64/python2.7/site-packages/symengine/lib/symengine_wrapper.so` we can see the latter strips debug info from the file.

So I would explicitly decourage using `make install` in the documentation for such noobs as myself.

issue_title: id3.CRM is useless and buggy
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Daniel Plachotich (Bitbucket: [danpla](http://bitbucket.org/danpla), GitHub: [danpla](http://github.com/danpla))**

----------------------------------------

When certain 2.2 tag is added to `ID3`, the `add()` method converts it to a 2.3/4 one by using name of the parent class. The problem is that `CRM` tag is derived directly from `Frame` and has no 2.3 analog.

Adding `CRM` tag (manually or by reading from a file) results the following:
```
#!python

>>> t = id3.ID3()
>>> t.add(id3.CRM())
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/mutagen/id3/__init__.py", line 353, in add
    return self.loaded_frame(frame)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/mutagen/id3/__init__.py", line 345, in loaded_frame
    tag = type(tag).__base__(tag)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/mutagen/id3/_frames.py", line 62, in __init__
    other._to_other(self)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/mutagen/id3/_frames.py", line 77, in _to_other
    raise ValueError
ValueError
>>> 
```
Because:

```
#!python

>>> id3.Frame()._framespec is not id3.CRM()._framespec
True
```
This means that no one uses `CRM` and it can be simply removed from Mutagen to avoid confusion. `ID3.add()`, however, must be fixed to ignore this tag.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lazka/mutagen/issue/239

issue_title: Am I misunderstanding arrays or is this a bug?
issue_body:
Consider the following SMT-Lib script:

http://rise4fun.com/Z3/LuRm4

    (declare-const x Int)
    (declare-const a1 (Array Int Int))
    (assert (= (select a1 0) 0))
    (assert (= (select a1 1) 1))
    (assert (= (select a1 2) 2))
    (assert (= (select a1 0) 2))
    (check-sat)

This is an unsatisfactory model as we are asserting that index 0 of a1 is both 0 and 2. This is expected.

Consider the following:

http://rise4fun.com/Z3/njxqM

    (declare-const x Int)
    (declare-const a1 (Array Int Int))
    (assert (= (select a1 x) x))
    (assert (= (select a1 2) 0))
    (check-sat)

This is a satisfactory model - why? We are asserting that an element at index x is equal to x - and as x has no conditions tied to it, we are essentially creating an array such as: [0] = 0, [1] = 1, [2] = 2 etc... But at the same time we are also asserting that element 2 is equal to 0.

Am I misunderstanding arrays or is this a bug? If I am misunderstanding them, are there any resources which may help me? The Z3 guide on rise4fun is a little sparse on these subjects, and the formal documentation on the ArraysEx theory is well over my head!

issue_title: Bug in maximize with forall constraints
issue_body:
Hello Z3 developers,

I was trying to find optimal bounds for a loop index, eg

for (i=5; i>=1; i--) { ... }

Minimal bug case is just the lower bound:

```
(declare-const x_min Int)
(assert (forall ((x Int)) (implies (<= 1 x) (<= x_min (- x 1)))))
(assert (<= x_min 5))
(maximize x_min)
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

Z3 4.6 returns 
```
sat
(model (define-fun x_min () Int 5))
```
which is wrong: x isn't bounded by 5.  However, changing the 1 to a 0, or any non-positive value I've tested:
```
(declare-const x_min Int)
(assert (forall ((x Int)) (implies (<= 0 x) (<= x_min (- x 1)))))
(assert (<= x_min 5))
(maximize x_min)
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```
This returns:
```
sat
(model (define-fun x_min () Int (- 1)))
```
which is correct.

Also, is this sort of case supposed to be supported, or should I expect it to fail / take exponential time in general?

issue_title: Recurrent layers: Accept layer for hid_init
issue_body:
The recurrent layers currently have some custom behaviour when `hid_init` is a TensorVariable rather than a shared variable, callable or numpy array: They assume `hid_init` is a tensor of one order higher than otherwise, to include the batch dimension, and they assume `hid_init` is not to be learned.

With #404, parameters can be arbitrary Theano expressions anyway, and overriding that behaviour to assume a different dimensionality is a bit awkward. As discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/11#issuecomment-139221388 and following, a better solution would be allowing `hid_init` to be a `Layer`, and assuming it would include the batch dimensionality in this case. This would also be a step towards supporting the encoder-decoder architecture discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/391#issuecomment-144549176 and following.

issue_title: The line 119 in Lasagne/lasagne/layers/merge.py has a bug
issue_body:
Line 119:
`slices_by_input = [[] for i in range(ndim)]`

Should be:
`slices_by_input = [[] for i in range(len(inputs))]`

ndim is the dimension of the tensors.
The ndim should be replaced with len(inputs) because each element in the list is corresponding to an input.

issue_title: Tests do not run with clean state of pynwb
issue_body:
## 1) Bug

Changes to the global state that is tracked in the global state variables __TYPE_MAP and __NS_CATALOG persists across tests.  This means that any tests that run after other tests that change the state (such as extension tests) are running with those changes.

The changing state can be observed by inserting:

    import pynwb
    logging.info("loaded namespaces %s", pynwb.get_type_map().namespace_catalog.namespaces)

issue_title: Support for F expressions in `in` lookup
issue_body:
This query will be processed incorrectly:

```python
Model.objects.filter(
    amount1__in=(Money(4, 'USD'), F('amount2'))
)
```

Actual query:

```SQL
SELECT "model"."id", "model"."amount1_currency", "model"."amount1", "model"."amount2_currency", "model"."amount2" FROM "model" WHERE (("model"."amount1_currency" = 'USD' AND "model"."amount1" = 4) OR ("model"."amount1" = ("model"."amount2")))
```

Expected:
```SQL
SELECT "model"."id", "model"."amount1_currency", "model"."amount1", "model"."amount2_currency", "model"."amount2" FROM "model" WHERE (("model"."amount1_currency" = 'USD' AND "model"."amount1" = 4) OR ("model"."amount1_currency" = ("model"."amount2_currency") AND "model"."amount1" = ("model"."amount2")))
```

issue_body:
ZeroRPC 0.4.4 depends on pyzmq==13.1.0. Python/setuptools don't handle "==" dependencies well - if a project depends on both pyzmq and zerorpc, and setuptools decides to download pyzmq first, it will install the newest pyzmq, and then fail when it reaches the installation of zerorpc...
I understand that the newest pyzmq breaks zerorpc, but I'm not sure what's the problem. Is it possible to adapt zerorpc and/or open an issue for pyzmq to fix their issues?
