issue_title: Incorrect handling of argument size when using USE_SIMPLIFIED_CCALLS
issue_body:
Note: I've rewritten this after digging deeper in the problem!

Here is a simple code which uses only 16 bits from the ESI register (assuming we are on X86) for a comparison:
```
0x0:	cmp	si, 0x7f
0x4:	jg	0x1d
```

If we translate it to VEX, we get the following.
```
IRSB {
   t0:Ity_I16 t1:Ity_I16 t2:Ity_I16 t3:Ity_I32 t4:Ity_I32 t5:Ity_I1 t6:Ity_I32 t7:Ity_I32 t8:Ity_I32 t9:Ity_I32 t10:Ity_I32 t11:Ity_I32

   00 | ------ IMark(0x0, 4, 0) ------
   01 | t2 = GET:I16(esi)
   02 | PUT(cc_op) = 0x00000005
   03 | t3 = 16Uto32(t2)
   04 | PUT(cc_dep1) = t3
   05 | PUT(cc_dep2) = 0x0000007f
   06 | PUT(cc_ndep) = 0x00000000
   07 | PUT(eip) = 0x00000004
   08 | ------ IMark(0x4, 2, 0) ------
   09 | t10 = x86g_calculate_condition(0x0000000e,0x00000005,t3,0x0000007f,0x00000000):Ity_I32
   10 | t5 = 32to1(t10)
   11 | if (t5) { PUT(eip) = 0x6; Ijk_Boring }
   NEXT: PUT(eip) = 0x0000001d; Ijk_Boring
}
```
Later in `simuvex/ccall.py` when `USE_SIMPLIFIED_CCALLS` option is specified, simuvex calls `pc_calculate_condition_simple` (added some comments about what happens in the function).

```python
   # Resolve the OP type, in this case it will be 'G_CC_OP_SUBW'
   op = data_inverted[platform]['OpTypes'][cc_op]
   # Extract the 'SUB' string from OP 
   op = op[8 : -1]
    # Extract the condition
   cond = [...]
   # Function to call, will be pc_actions_SUB_CondLE
   funcname = "pc_actions_%s_%s" % (op, cond)
   if funcname in globals():
       r = globals()[funcname](state, cc_dep1, cc_dep2, cc_ndep)
       return state.se.Concat(state.se.BVV(0, state.arch.bits - 1), r), []
```

The problem with this code is that it doesnt care about the size of the operation, which in this case is WORD (16bits), and it will run the same function for:
 - G_CC_OP_SUBB
 - G_CC_OP_SUBW
 - G_CC_OP_SUBL

Another question: maybe I understand the concepts wrong, but shouldn't be `0x6` the default exit, and `0x1d` the conditional one?

issue_body:

I'm still experimenting with ```pngpixel``` but I have encountered a weird behaviour even if I'm able to generate the path between the ```main``` and ```png_malloc```. The problem is that when ```png_malloc_base``` is called ```malloc`` should be also called few instructions after:

The symbolic execution can correctly reach that point:

```
Block starting @0x100b970L

issue_title: extracting constraints in smt2 format for z3
issue_body:
I am trying to extract all the constraints passed to the **z3 solver** even in case of timeout, exception, etc. I thus considered the `backend_z3` and in particular the `__batch_eval()` function. I want the output in a **smt2 syntax** so that I can then process the extracted constraints with the standalone version of **z3**.

I thus added the following code in `__batch_eval()` just before the end of the function.

```
            smt_file = open("/results/be-query.smt2", 'a')
            mysolver = solver
            mysolver.add(self._op_raw_Not(self._op_raw_And(*[(ex == ex_v) for ex, ex_v in zip(exprs, r)])))
            f = mysolver
            v = (z3.Ast * 0)()
            if isinstance(f, z3.Solver):
                # print "solver is an instance of z3.Solver"
                a = f.assertions()
                if len(a) == 0:
                    f = z3.BoolVal(True)
                else:
                    f = z3.And(*a)
            z3.Z3_set_ast_print_mode(f.ctx_ref(), z3.Z3_PRINT_SMTLIB2_COMPLIANT)
            ste_expr = z3.Z3_benchmark_to_smtlib_string(f.ctx_ref(), "", "QF_AUFBV", None, "", 0, v, f.as_ast())
            smt_file.write(ste_expr)
            smt_file.write('\n')
            smt_file.close()
```

I tried it with a set of binaries and it works pretty well. The extracted smt2 files can be easily used with the standalone version of z3.
 
Unfortunately, extending my test with a large dataset of real malware, I found that I do not always have a consistent behaviour. A large number of binary (about 75%) do not produce any smt2 file. In other cases, when the smt2 file is generated, re-running angr on the same binary: I could have no file or the produced smt2 files differ significantly.

issue_title: RPCError only reports first error (Junos)
issue_body:
I am using ncclient with a Junos system.  During a commit cycle, a number of rpc-error elements are generated.  For example:

````xml
<rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" xmlns:junos="http://xml.juniper.net/junos/12.3R3/junos">
<commit-results>
   <rpc-error>
      <error-severity>warning</error-severity>
      <error-path>[edit routing-instances charlie instance-type]</error-path>
      <error-info>
         <bad-element>instance-type virtual-router</bad-element>
      </error-info>
      <error-message>mgd: requires 'virtual-router' license</error-message>
   </rpc-error>
   <rpc-error>
      <error-severity>warning</error-severity>
      <error-path>[edit routing-instances charlie protocols]</error-path>
      <error-info>
         <bad-element>ospf</bad-element>
      </error-info>
      <error-message>mgd: requires 'ospf2' license</error-message>
   </rpc-error>
   <rpc-error>
      <error-severity>error</error-severity>
      <source-daemon>
      eswd
      </source-daemon>
      <error-message>
      No vlan matches vlan tag 200 for interface ge-0/0/1.0
      </error-message>
   </rpc-error>
   <rpc-error>
      <error-severity>error</error-severity>
      <error-message>
         configuration check-out failed
      </error-message>
   </rpc-error>
   </commit-results>
</rpc-reply>
````

The "bug" is that ncclient is only reporting the first `<rpc-error>`, which in this case happens to be a error-severity=warning.  So the calling program is not able to report on the error-severity=error items.

Can this be easily resolved?  I don't know if this issue is specific to the Junos module in ncclient or not.  

I appreciate your time to investigate and provide guidance.  I can attempt to make changes with your guidance and issue pull request when I have a fix; if needed/etc.

Thank you!
-- Jeremy

issue_title: Exception on terminating a session
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm hitting the following exception quite regularly, with hit rate around 10%.
```
Exception in thread session (most likely raised during interpreter shutdown):
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 801, in __bootstrap_inner
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/ncclient/transport/ssh.py", line 575, in run
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/ncclient/transport/ssh.py", line 312, in close
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/paramiko/transport.py", line 699, in close
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/paramiko/transport.py", line 1662, in stop_thread
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 951, in join
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 364, in wait
<type 'exceptions.ValueError'>: list.remove(x): x not in list
```

Here's my code:
```
def main():
    try:
        with manager.connect(
            host="localhost",
            port=2022,
            username=XXX
            password=XXX
            timeout=5,
            hostkey_verify=False,
            look_for_keys=False,
            allow_agent=False,
        ) as connection:
           # do stuff
    except ncclient.NCClientError:
        print("Error")
```

I'm using ncclient version 0.5.3 on Ubuntu 16.04.

AFAICS, the problem is that ncclient is maintaining its own transport thread in addition to the transport thread used by paramiko. 

While paramiko is well behaved and has logic to ensure that it closes all its threads before it exits, ncclient has no such logic and relies on a transport failure to signal the close of the thread and to tidy up synchronously.  What I believe needs to happen is that ncclient should wait for the thread to close.  For example, inside SSHSession.close():
```
       while self.is_alive() and (self is not threading.current_thread()):
            self.join(10)
```

I've tested that with this code change I'm not able to hit the exception anymore.

Are you happy with the fix?  Would you like me to submit a pull request?

Thanks,
Michael

issue_title: Move channel_override examples out of simple_goto and small_demo

According to @tridge overriding channels is intended for simulating user input (ie in autotest) or for implementing joystick behaviour. It should not be used to directly drive a vehicle, except as a last resort. Instead the appropriate mavlink commands should be used.

The following examples set the channel_override in  a way that we would not recommend:

* https://github.com/diydrones/dronekit-python/blob/master/example/small_demo/small_demo.py#L80
> https://github.com/diydrones/dronekit-python/blob/master/example/simple_goto/simple_goto.py#L26

I propose that we:

* strip this from small_demo and simple_goto, and create a small independent demo that shows just this behaviour. 
* add a warning in the new example's code and docs about this use case
* replace the throttle setting in the simple_goto with a more appropriate command for setting the throttle.

issue_title: API explanations in the top level Vehicle or convenience classes?
issue_body:
If you have an attribute like ``vehicle.heading`` which represents a single value there is just one place where it can be documented.

For a compound object like ``Vehicle.location`` you can have the documentation in the top level attribute or spread in the sub classes like ``Locations``, ``LocationGlobal``, ``LocationGlobalRelative`` etc. 

Having most of the docs up in the top level ``vehicle`` makes sense because you mostly operate on the attributes through this object, but it does mean that you can't easily scan through ``Vehicle`` to see the available attributes and then dig down for functionality. This wouldn't matter so much if there was a class listing in the sidebar (as requested) but it is a bit of a pain as we don't.

Most of all though, it is important to try be consistent. My leaning is to have most of the documents in the top level ``Vehicle``, with minimal documentation in "sub attributes" (and hope our docs infrastructure improves).

@tcr3dr @mrpollo Do you have any opinion on this?

issue_title: reserved characters are treated inconsistently and not sensibly preserved
issue_body:
This has been a design flaw since the inception of the library, so, mea culpa on that.

Fundamentally, preserving, escaping, and encoding "reserved" characters is entirely the URL object's job, and it's failing at that.  Possibly the most succinct demonstration of the problem is this:

```python
>>> u = URL()
>>> u = u.child(u'/')
>>> u = u.asIRI()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    u = u.asIRI()
  File
"/Users/glyph/Library/Python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/hyperlink/_url.py",
line 1116, in to_iri
    fragment=_percent_decode(self.fragment))
  File
"/Users/glyph/Library/Python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/hyperlink/_url.py",
line 861, in replace
    userinfo=_optional(userinfo, self.userinfo),
  File
"/Users/glyph/Library/Python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/hyperlink/_url.py",
line 606, in __init__
    for segment in path))
  File
"/Users/glyph/Library/Python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/hyperlink/_url.py",
line 606, in <genexpr>
    for segment in path))
  File
"/Users/glyph/Library/Python/2.7/lib/python/site-packages/hyperlink/_url.py",
line 410, in _textcheck
    % (''.join(delims), name, value))
ValueError: one or more reserved delimiters /?# present in path segment: u'/'
>>>
```

This is - obviously I hope - the wrong place to be failing with an error like this.

There was previously *some* attempt to preserve these characters in the data model and escape them only upon stringification, but d26814c074c6f9787e62af907df17fbd68fde615 wrecked these semantics.  (In fairness: the attempt to do this was broken, and there are some places, like the scheme, where certain characters indeed *cannot* be represented, so this direction isn't entirely wrong.)

Fundamentally if a user wants to encode slashes, question marks, hash signs or whatever else that a human might, for example, type into a text field, then it should be possible to do that.

We could fix this obvious manifestation of the problem by just putting back the escape-only-on-`asText` logic, but that still leaves an even more pernicious problem:

```python
>>> u = URL(path=tuple([u'%2525']))
>>> u.asText()
u'%2525'
>>> u.asIRI().asText()
u'%25'
>>> u.asIRI().asIRI().asText()
u'%'
>>> 
```

Clearly, multiple trips through `asIRI` should not be un-escaping the escape character - the idea is that `.asIRI()` is a normalization step, that should be idempotent upon subsequent calls.

For the moment, I'm not sure exactly what the correct fix is here, but the property I'd really like to preserve is that for any `x`,

`URL.fromText(URL().child(x).<as many asIRI()s or asURI()s as you want>.asText()).<as many .asIRI()s as you want, although possibly not .asURI()s>.segments[0] == x`

python/GangaDirac/test/Unit/Utilities/TestDiracUtilities.py:28: AssertionError
```

I suggest the best solution to this is to intentionally add a line which is something like `---Ganga-Execute-Cut-Here---` which would allow for any output bleeding in from initializing the environment to be correctly removed from the result which is an object we want. This can probably be done within Ganga as the 2 steps of initialize and execute are well separated in the code so this should just be a matter of writing to a stream correctly.

issue_title: wraparound in pywt.swt
issue_body:
When performing swt using a Haar wavelet, the last index in the L-filter is calculated as 1/sqrt(2) * (index[-1] + index[0]), i.e. it seems as if the function wraps around. Is this intentional? I'm certainly no expert on wavelet transforms. Here is the output from some simple tests I did in jupyter:

Test1, length = 8
In: [5 4 3 0 0 0 0 0]
Out-H: [ 6.364   4.9497  2.1213  0.      0.      0.      0.      3.5355]
Out-L: [ 0.7071  0.7071  2.1213  0.      0.      0.      0.     -3.5355]

Test2, length = 6
In: [5 4 3 0 0 0]
Out-H: [ 6.364   4.9497  2.1213  0.      0.      3.5355]
Out-L: [ 0.7071  0.7071  2.1213  0.      0.     -3.5355]

Test3, reverse
In: [0 0 0 0 0 3 4 5]
Out-H: [ 0.      0.      0.      0.      2.1213  4.9497  6.364   3.5355]
Out-L: [ 0.      0.      0.      0.     -2.1213 -0.7071 -0.7071  3.5355]

Test4, reverse with extra padding
In: [0 0 0 0 0 3 4 5 0 0]
Out-H: [ 0.      0.      0.      0.      2.1213  4.9497  6.364   3.5355  0.      0.    ]
Out-L: [ 0.      0.      0.      0.     -2.1213 -0.7071 -0.7071  3.5355  0.      0.    ]

Test5, discreet instead of stationary wavelet transform
In: [5 4 3 0 0 0 0 0]
Out-H: [ 6.364   2.1213  0.      0.    ]
Out-L: [ 0.7071  2.1213  0.      0.    ]

3.5355 is equal to 1/sqrt(2) * 5

issue_title: LibRealSense vs Win-DCM-RealSense distortion model
issue_body:
We are using SR300.

The distortion parameters returned by librealsense's rs_get_stream_intrinsics() ("Inverse Brown Conrady" model) are different from the parameters returned by the Windows DCM driver. They are not the same, nor do the DCM parameters match the "Forward Brown Conrady" parameters.

I suppose this is intentional and they use different distortion models? This was implied by **Wollimayer** in [Issue 46](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues/46).

Is there a way to retrieve the parameters of the Windows DCM distortion model using librealsense? Or maybe there's a known function that translates between the models? 
We want to substitute code that uses these parameters and that depends on the DCM with librealsense.

**Note 1:** To the best of our knowledge, the DCM distortion model is the same as the OpenCV distortion model, which is also the same as the Matlab Computer Vision Toolbox distortion model. Seems like someone might have dealt with these parameter translations before.

**Note 2:** We do get the same 3x3 projection matrix in both APIs.

**Note 3:** We tested with two SR300, one is an older development model and one is a commercial model from Creative.

| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model |  SR300  | 
| Firmware Version |  3.21.0.0 |

| Kernel Version (Linux Only)  |   |

issue_title: Call a model's method when querying for a field
Cannot return null for non-nullable field FooType.barType [Enum]
issue_body:
The error occurs with graphene-django==2.0.0 with a field with choices, for example:
```
class Foo(models.Model):
    ONE = 1
    TWO = 2
  
    BAR_TYPES = (
        (ONE, _('one'),
        (TWO, _('two'),
    )

    bar_type = models.PositiveIntegerField(_('type'), choices=BAR_TYPES)


class FooType(DjangoObjectType):                                    
    class Meta:
        model = Foo
```
This error can be resolved with an explicit graphene.Field declaration: 
```
class FooEnum(graphene.Enum):
    ONE = 1
    TWO = 2                       


class FooType(DjangoObjectType):                                    
    bar_type = graphene.Field(FooEnum)
    class Meta:
        model = Foo
```
Is it really necessary to duplicate the choices, though?

issue_title: Travis and AppVeyor may fail at different times
issue_body:
If the standalone binary build fails during Travis CI processing, it will fail in the "deploy" job/stage but in the `script` step. If the standalone binary build fails during AppVeyor processing, it will fail in the `before_deploy` step (which only occurs if deployment conditions have been met). 

Both should fail prior to deployment because deployment does not always occur. However, if the standalone binary does not properly build, the build should fail even if deployment is not going to happen. While the standalone binary isn't strictly needed unless deployment will take place, building the standalone binary is a valuable test and let's us know as early in the process as possible that there are problems and that deployment won't be possible later without fixes.

In addition, for maintainability, keeping Travis and AppVeyor as logically close in functionality as possible is helpful.

issue_title: load_services undocumented
issue_body:
I really had a hard time finding out how `PluginGlobals.load_services()` works - I didn't make it. Is there any kind of documentation besides the few lines in the [pdf](https://software.sandia.gov/trac/pyutilib/export/1831/pyutilib.component.doc/trunk/doc/plugin/pca.pdf]?

AFAIK, and after having worked myself through the code of the loader, you have to import a Loader class first which implements `IPluginLoader` *before* calling `load_services()`.
So:
```python
# plugin.py
class Test:
    implements(ITest)
    def test():
        print("test)
```

```python
# main.py
class ITest(Interface)
    def test():
        pass

class Main:
    test_extension = ExtensionPoint(ITest)
    
    def __init__(self):    
        loader = ImportLoader()
        PluginGlobals.get_env().load_services(path="./plugins", auto_disable=False)

    # test it:
    for ep in self.test_extension:
        ep.test()
```

* Does this work (here test(9 is never called, the for loop does not iterate at all over the plugins)?
* Is the `get_env()` necessary (the pdf doc doesn't mention it)?
* even if I write `auto_disable=False` the plugin is not called in the following context.
Is there any chance that the documentation gets updated?

issue_title: Travis is testing pyexcel-0.2.1
issue_body:
Before 0.2.1 was cut, Travis tests on master had the following results in `pip freeze`

```
pyexcel-io==0.2.0
pyexcel-ods3==0.2.0
pyexcel-xls==0.2.0
pyexcel-xlsx==0.2.0
```

https://travis-ci.org/pyexcel/pyexcel/jobs/126440816#L311 (87ec8c7)

After 0.2.1 was cut, Travis tests on master had the following results in `pip freeze`

```
pyexcel==0.2.1
pyexcel-io==0.2.0
pyexcel-ods3==0.2.0
pyexcel-text==0.2.0
pyexcel-xls==0.2.0
pyexcel-xlsx==0.2.0
```

https://travis-ci.org/pyexcel/pyexcel/jobs/126588417#L319 (badecbf)

The problem is that b0146294 (Apr 29 2016) added `pyexcel-text>=0.2.0` to `tests/requirements.txt` , and pyexcel-text 0.2.0 depends on pyexcel 2.1.0, so pip installs it from pypi before running the tests.

The very simple solution is for travis to re-install pyexcel[master] before running the tests.

However there is a larger problem of dependency management.

If we change the dependencies of `pyexcel` , pyexcel installed pyexcel-text[master], which
installed pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi), the old dependencies of pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi) are installed, which means we cant test on Travis with reduced/alternative dependencies, unless we forcibly uninstall them after `rnd_requirements.txt` and `tests/requirements.txt`.

An example of this is #38, where I want to downgrade texttable on Python 2.  pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi) will install texttable 0.8.2, whereas I want to install texttable>=0.8.1 .  When I install texttable 0.8.1 using apt, pip upgrades it to texttable 0.8.2, so it wont be tested.

Another related problem is that master is not incompatible with pyexcel-io 0.1.0, yet the requirements still show pyexcel-io>=0.1.0 .

And there is one solution for all of those.
Firstly, to install `pyexcel-text[master]`, we need to also install `.` at the same time.  However bugs in pip/setuptools means that alone doesnt work.  First we need to install everything in `rnd_requirements.txt` *without dependencies*.  That installs the master of `-text`, `-io`, etc.
Then reprocess it with dependencies and include `.` .  Patch coming shortly...

issue_title: csvr does not make the same assumptions about mmap availability as _compact.py
issue_body:
https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-io/blob/3f1d14ad80cf0aa0357efd9f20a6f22f51737511/pyexcel_io/readers/csvr.py#L234

Can we check if it's running on App Engine as well as PY27 and above? From the docs, the environment variable SERVER_SOFTWARE will report one of two things when running on App Engine: In the development web server, this value is "Development/X.Y" where "X.Y" is the version of the runtime. issue_title: Version number should be in a single place
issue_body:
Currently when we change the version number, this has to be done in 4 places:
- setup.py
- cmd2/cmd2.py
- tests/test_cmd2.py
- docs/conf.py

We should re-architect how the version is done so it only needs to be changed in a single place.  Or worst-case possibly in two places.  Having to change it in 4 places is painful and potentially prone to error.

issue_title: Converting variant types
issue_body:
Hey there! I am in the middle of replacing Boost.Python with pybind11 in my project and stumbled on this situation:

I have a C++ class with an instance field of type `boost::variant<A, B>`, where `A` and `B` are simple types that I have already exposed to python with `pybind11::_class` as `pyA` and `pyB`, respectively. `boost::variant` is basically a safe `union` type, i.e., it represents a single value that is guaranteed to be either `A` or `B`.

My question is: What is the recommended way to convert `boost::variant<A, B>` to `pyA` or `pyB` (as appropriate given the variant's concrete value)? How can I make sure that both `pyA` and `pyB` can be converted to `boost::variant<A, B>`?

The documentation points me towards using a custom `type_caster<boost::variant<A, B>>`, but it seems I would have to decide for _one_ corresponding Python type to translate to/from.

Any help is much appreciated.

issue_title: Negative strides in Python buffer view?
issue_body:
I have a C++ image type that allows negative strides. Python numpy arrays do too, so I was hoping I'd be able to convert between the two without data copy. But pybind11's `buffer_info` struct defines the strides with a `std::vector<size_t>`. In the code, Python's stride values are simply cast to unsigned:

    strides[i] = (size_t) view->strides[i];

This is bound to be problematic!

From Python's user manual (https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/buffer.html): 
>> Stride values can be any integer. For regular arrays, strides are usually positive, but a consumer MUST be able to handle the case `strides[n] <= 0`

I didn't have time to comb through the pybind11 code, are there any checks for negative strides?

Would it be OK if I tried to patch pybind11 to use signed strides?

issue_body:
vdirsyncer, version 0.16.3, Fedora 27

According to https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/vdir.html#metadata `color` contains the ascii hex code for a color and `displayname` the name to be used for display. Since decent editors should be used to maintain these files, they will usually have a final newline.
This final newline is ignored in `color`, but it is taken into account in `displayname`.

I suggest to always ignore a trailing newline.

issue_title: acme library crashes processing Unicode strings in ACME error detail
issue_labels: [{'id': 277074172, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20acme', 'name': 'area: acme', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 272789317, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20error%20handling', 'name': 'area: error handling', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954787, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
Based on https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/unicodeencodeerror-running-the-certbot/32422, I investigated a bit and concluded that the `acme` library is unsafe when the CA returns an error detail string containing non-ASCII characters; it crashes with a `UnicodeEncodeError` at

https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/master/acme/acme/messages.py#L39

(Imagine that `err` is `u"Errör message"`; calling `str()` on that is an error.)

In this case, a user had a 404 page containing Russian text and Certbot appeared to crash at the line above when trying to report a failed `webroot` authentication. I'm not certain that this is the only thing preventing Certbot from handling such an error properly, but if I've understood the problem properly, it's at least one obstacle.

issue_title: renew function does not call installer.deploy_cert()
issue_body:
The `renew` function does not call `installer.deploy_cert()` on every renewal. While this is expected behavior, there are also use-cases where it is desired the installer does handle/process the certificate on renewal. Use-cases are coping certs into a java keystore or push them to some other location/database where they are used. Not all application can work with (sym)linking to the certificates/keystore certbot provides itself.

Of course this kind or processing can also be handled by a wrapper script and/or by using the hooks available in `renew` command. But it would be nicer if this is possible within the plugin infrastructure itself, as it is very likely this code would be very similar to the deploy_cert() code when a new certificate is requested and it is better to keep similar processing in the same place.

We propose to introduce a `renew_deploy_cert()` in the `IInstaller` interface, and call this once a certificate is renewed. Standard the implementation should be empty, to keep the same behavior as in the current situation. But now it's up to the plugin developer to decide if they do or don't want to handle the new certificate in some manner. 

- tested against 0.9.3
- tested on 64-bit/debian jessie

(patch pending, testing against 0.11.0.dev.0 pending)

issue_title: Revoke old certificate on renewal
issue_body:
Currently `certbot v0.8.1` does not revoke the previous certificate upon renewal with `certbot renew`. I have checked the source code a little bit and also verified this by testing using the actual client. I believe this behavior has not been implemented because it can cause problems with certain automation setups, however, I think it should be implemented and initially be available using a flag, and then, maybe, at some point, be the default. 

Some comments on the proposal above:
* From a security perspective, it makes sense to have the old certificate revoked, since it would not be used any more and there's no reason to have it as valid.
* Most (if not all) browsers do not check for revocation information ([Can you visit this?](https://revoked.grc.com/)) therefore this might not make any difference currently.
* Since most people currently use `certbot renew`, the amount of revoked certificates will increase, placing further load in any CRLs / OCSP Servers that may be used by the CA. Currently the percentage of revoked certificates [is 0.4% for Let's Encrypt](https://plot.ly/~letsencrypt/9/daily-activity/#plot).
* If someone fails to reload their web server after the renewal, they will theoretically serve revoked certificates until they do. 

This is not a high priority issue, it is actually a feature request, and very few people will use this initially, however I am posting this issue so we can track the progress of this.

issue_title: NGINX error and use of  snippets suggesttion
Certbot in docker is broken when run on Alpine OS
issue_body:
I've spent the last few weeks debugging this on and off and think I've pinned it down now.

Any docker image that builds or installs certbot *during the image build process* (e.g inside the Dockerfile), including the official image, will fail to run certbot correctly if that image is then run on docker on Alpine Linux. All you will get when attempting to get a certificate is:

An unexpected error occurred:
SystemError: error return without exception set

(end of letsencrypt.log):
2018-03-13 15:51:14,948:DEBUG:acme.client:Sending GET request to https://acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org/directory.
2018-03-13 15:51:14,949:DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:Starting new HTTPS connection (1): acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org


To be explicit, this is Alpine as the base OS on the machine/VM, with docker running on it, which is then running an Alpine based container:

Machine -> Alpine 3.7 OS -> Docker 17.12.1-ce -> Container (alpine:3.7 based)

If the *same image* is run on docker on Centos7 (my other available test system), then certbot runs and gets certificates perfectly fine.

Now, the mind-bending part (and a reason this took so long to investigate) is if you build certbot in a running container manually, on Alpine OS, with the *exact same commands* that are in the dockerfile, it works fine(!).

So if you build this (on Centos or Alpine, doesn't matter) and run it on docker on Alpine, certbot will fail with the above error whenever you try to get a certificate:

```
FROM alpine:3.7
RUN apk --update --no-cache add python2 augeas gcc python2-dev musl-dev libffi-dev openssl-dev py2-pip
WORKDIR /root/
RUN wget https://github.com/certbot/certbot/archive/v0.22.0.tar.gz && tar -xzf ./v0.22.0.tar.gz
WORKDIR /root/certbot-0.22.0
RUN pip install ./
CMD ["sh"]
```

(deliberately bad/verbose Dockerfile to illustrate the point)

But if you build it inside a container manually with the same commands, running on Alpine OS, it works:

```
docker run -dit -p 80:80 --name certmanual alpine:3.7
docker exec -it certmanual sh
apk update
apk add python2 augeas gcc python2-dev musl-dev libffi-dev openssl-dev py2-pip
cd /root/
wget https://github.com/certbot/certbot/archive/v0.22.0.tar.gz
tar -xzf ./v0.22.0.tar.gz
cd certbot-0.22.0
pip install ./
```

I was using "certbot certonly standalone" throughout my tests.

I'd like to build my own certbot images and run them on Alpine, so it would be great to know what's going on!

Thanks

issue_title: Change log rotation scheme
issue_body:
Right now the log rotation scheme hardcodes retention of 10 logs, with no provision to configure more. We should either:

1) Add a flag for log retention duration, or
2) Remove custom log rotation

I prefer (2), because it allows the normal OS logrotate mechanism to work. I recently had this issue on a test machine where I configured logrotate so it would preserve logs for longer. This appeared to work, since I had a large number of logs in /var/log/letsencrypt. However, some logs were getting removed by certbot's auto-removal before logrotate had a chance to preserve them. I believe that it's always the case that certbot's log rotation + logrotate will lose at least one log with every run of certbot, in the steady state.

issue_title: Renew only renewed partially, claims other certificates are not due for renewal
issue_body:
Recently I received e-mails from Letsencrypt regarding certificate expiration. Because I have certbot set up as a cron script with the 'renew' argument, some of my certificates in the fullchain were renewed, yet I noticed that some other certificates in the fullchain simply refused to renew. Even when I ran the command manually, I would get the following:
```
tiago@yyyyyyyy:/usr/local/certbot$ sudo /usr/local/certbot/certbot-auto renew

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/xxxxxxxx.com.conf
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following certs are not due for renewal yet:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/xxxxxxxx.com/fullchain.pem (skipped)
No renewals were attempted
```

This was really strange, since some of those certificates would expire soon, on October 1st and certbot didn't realize this. In the end I manually forced it with ```--force-renewal``` and all the certificates were renewed (less than ideal, since now my other certificates which had also recently been renewed, got renewed once more - not a problem, more of a nuisance).

Anyone else faced this issue? Is it a bug or just a problem in my configs?

issue_title: [META] Use more specific exceptions for the reasons a transactions may fail
issue_body:
### Problem definition

To have better error handling and reporting Raiden should have specific exceptions for the different causes of transaction failures.

Example, currently [ChannelIncorrectStateError](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/ea29dff47d84ceb80154694755dbe556b31eaba8/raiden/exceptions.py#L131) is used for [deposit](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/ea29dff47d84ceb80154694755dbe556b31eaba8/raiden/network/proxies/token_network.py#L509-L512) and [close](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/ea29dff47d84ceb80154694755dbe556b31eaba8/raiden/network/proxies/token_network.py#L563-L565) failures, on both cases there is no distinction if the channel never existed or if it was just closed. The first case is a bug because the identifier never existed, while the second may be a recoverable race condition among close and deposit.

### Transactions failures which are unrecoverable

General transactions failures which are bugs, and should not be handled:

- [ ] Transaction removed from the pool:
  - [ ] The nonce was invalid (too-low, too-high). Considered a bug when local signing is used.
  - [ ] The gas was invalid (lower than the minimum)
  - [ ] Another transaction overwrote it (same nonce, higher gas). Considered a bug if we are not trying to increase the probability of the transaction being mined (which is the case as of https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/commit/d329583e492798e558f84ac1d839f345384d231d).
- [ ] The transaction had data and the `to` address never had bytecode
- [ ] The transaction failed because the `start_gas` was too low [OOG]
- [ ] A transaction which hit an assert

Smart contract transactions which are bugs and should not be handled (this assumes https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/1965):

- [x] Invalid operations on the token network:
   - [x] Open while a non-settled channel exists (as of https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/commit/585f8a014a869eb3e5e0983c54fb1ccaa4cc6144 which disallows multiple channels for the same participants)
- [ ] Invalid operations on the netting channel:
   - [x] Deposit, Withdraw, or Close for a channel that was never opened
   - [x] Deposit, Withdraw, or Close for a channel that was settled
    - [x] Deposit or Withdraw which fails because the monotonic value decreased, because such operations are disallowed by the Raiden node.
   - [x] updateTransfer for a channel that was never closed
   - [ ] close with an invalid balance proof hash
   - [ ] updateTransfer with an invalid balance proof hash
   - [ ] settle with invalid balance proof data
   - [x] settle for a channel that was never closed (this assumes the settlement window is larger than the nº of confirmation blocks)
   - [x] settle for a channel that was closed but the settlement window is not over
   - [x] unlock while the channel was never settled (as of https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/commit/585f8a014a869eb3e5e0983c54fb1ccaa4cc6144 which blinds the data from the monitoring services and required the settle to reveal the merkle tree root)

#### Transactions failures which are recoverable

General transactions failures which are races, and should be handled:

- [ ] The transaction start gas was higher than the block gas limit
- [ ] A transaction to a smart contract address which has selfdestructed
- [ ] A transaction which hit a revert

General smart contract transaction which are races, and should be handled:

- [x] Race operations on the secret registry:
  - [x] The secret is already registered
- [x] Race operations on the token registry:
  - [x] The token is already registered
- [x] Race operations on the token network:
  - [x] An open which fails because the channel is already open
- [x] Race operations on the netting channel:
  - [x] A deposit or withdraw which fails because the channel is closed
  - [x] A close fails because the channel is already closed
  - [x] A settle fails because the channel is already settled
  - [ ] An unlock fails because that participant channel state is already unlocked

### Tasks

- [x] Add two base exceptions `RaidenRecoverableError` and `RaidenUnrecoverableError`
- [ ] Individually implement the specific transactions, which should inherit from `RaidenRecoverableError` or `RaidenUnrecoverableError`, and raise from the smart contract proxies.
- [x] The Raiden event handler must handle `RaidenRecoverableError` by logging and dispatching a state change to clean up the transaction queue (#1950).

Right now stdout and stderr of cell output is collected and displayed after the cell is completed. If we execute

```
   import time
   for i in range(20):
        time.sleep(1)
        print(i)
```
in Python 3 kernel (not in SoS), it would print a line for every 1 second. If we execute it in SoS, it will display all 20 lines after 20 seconds. We should perhaps try to improve this.

issue_title: Notebook workflow with non-ascii characters
issue_body: Right now we use `btoa` to convert workflow collected from frontend to ascii and pass it to the backend with magic `%frontend`. However, this function will fail if the workflow contains non-ascii characters. We should try to pass the workflow in another method.

issue_title: Interrupted `sos execute -q` command leaves falsely submitted jobs
issue_body: I interrupted a `sos execute task_id -q hpc` command which was obviously submitting a job to the PBS queue. The job shows as pending by sos but it never was sent to PBS, and would not be sent to PBS anyway. This is a rare case but we should perhaps improve status detection and/or provide ways to detection or cleanup this case.

issue_title: Feature / Let measurement class register a ParamSpec
PyQTgraph Plotting API hard to add legend
issue_body:
I want to add a legend to a plot by doing something similar to the example below 

```python 
from qcodes.plots.pyqtgraph import QtPlot
vw = QtPlot(windowTitle='My Plot') # vw stands for viewing window 
# a is some analysis object that contains my data, labels and legend
for i in range(4): 
    vw.add(x=a.sweep_points, y=a.measured_values[i], 
                xlabel=a.xlabel, ylabel='Contrast',  symbol='o', symbolSize=5, 
              name=labels[i])  
vw.subplots[0].addLegend() # this acts on the pyqtgraph object directly and is not part of the qcodes API
```
The above example does not work. Instead I have to hack my way around it and use the following snippet 

```python 
vw.clear()
vw.subplots = [vw.add_subplot()]
vw.subplots[0].addLegend()
for i in range(4): 
    vw.add(x=a.sweep_points, y=a.measured_values[i], xlabel=a.xlabel, ylabel='Contrast',  symbol='o', symbolSize=5, 
           name=labels[i])
``` 
This is undesirable because 
- it is hard to add a Legend
- I need to know the internals of the subplot object and why I need to expand the trace first (by hand). 
- the legend itself looks visually bad (gray background spaces in beginning are ignored). 
It results in the following image. 
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6142932/22643086/62690a84-ec5d-11e6-8396-9b351aa86f8c.png)

I propose improving the plotting API by adding a simple addLegend() function. 
@nataliejpg , I believe you also had some suggestions to improve the API. Let's collect them in this issue.

issue_title: max_size vocab is not consistent.
issue_body:
**Context:**
Num field includes the numbers 0 - 9. I set `max_size=10`. Then I print the vocab that was built:

```
    num_field.build_vocab(train, max_size=10)
    print(num_field.vocab.itos)
    # ['<unk>', '<pad>', '<s>', '</s>', u'1', u'2']
    print(len(num_field.vocab.itos))
    # 6
```

Then I checked the `words` created from tokenization:
```
print(words)
# [(u'1', 11308), (u'2', 11270), (u'9', 11058), (u'0', 11020), (u'5', 10952), (u'4', 10942), (u'6', 10914), (u'8', 10820), (u'3', 10766), (u'7', 10706), ('</s>', 0), ('<pad>', 0), ('<s>', 0), ('<unk>', 0)]
```

Looks like the vocab built includes only 6 tokens yet the max_size is 10 while there are 14 possible tokens.

**Problem:**
If the number of tokens is larger than `max_size`, `build_vocab` does not fill up the vocabulary up till `max_size`.

**Possible Solution:**
Update https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/vocab.py#L129 to not subtract `len(self.itos)` from `max_size`.

issue_title: Support custom config file locations
issue_body:
Add the option for multiple config files, which can themselves be .gitignored. The link between a stack and its config file is stored somewhere in the `.pulumi` folder. See design notes at [Simplifying our config story](https://github.com/pulumi/design/blob/master/meetings/2018/2018-01-25.md).

A few details:
- For a given stack, config can be either in `Pulumi.yaml` or a custom config file, but not both

Open question:
- What happens when a stack is deleted via `stack rm`? Is the config removed from the custom file, similar to Pulumi.yaml, or is there an additional interactive question?

issue_title: Mark actors as removed when the corresponding driver process exits.
issue_body:
Currently, when an actor runs the `__ray_terminate__` task, it marks itself as removed in Redis. This prevents the unnecessary recreation of that actor if its corresponding local scheduler dies. We need to do the same thing when the corresponding driver dies.

At the very least we should check the driver ID when recreating actors so that we don't recreate actors for drivers that are no longer around.

issue_title: What should the behavior of ray.init be when it is called multiple times?
issue_body:
`ray.init` currently throws an exception the second time it is called. However, it can be pretty common to call it multiple times (e.g., in an jupyter notebook where it is common to rerun cells).

There are a couple options.
- Throw an exception.
- Do nothing and print a warning.
- If it is called with the same arguments that were called the first time, do nothing, otherwise throw an exception.
- Kill everything from the previous `ray.init` and run it again.

issue_title: Add a fallback mechanism
issue_body:
Right now `LocalizedValue` falls back to the primary language if no translation for the current language is present. I think it's interesting if the user could specify a fallback chain. This could be in a per-missing-language fashion:

```
LOCALIZED_FIELDS_FALLBACKS = {
    'en': ['es', 'eu'],
    'es': ['eu', 'en'],
    'eu': ['es', 'en'],
}
```

For backwards compatibility, `LOCALIZED_FIELDS_FALLBACKS.get(lang, [PRIMARY_LANGUAGE])`.

Alternatively, the setting could be a function that is given the missing language and field info.

This could be a global setting or a field parameter.

issue_title: Usage of API and documentation for projection metadata in las files
issue_body:
I have hesitated to ask this question, because I don't know if this is the correct forum to ask questions that are mainly usage related. However, I have decided that this question is both an API issue and documentation issue so is relevant to ask here.

A common problem for people working on lidar datasets is rasterizing point clouds.  Documenting how to pull out the projection information from each las file version would make it much easier to write code that can rasterize a point cloud. 

This is a feature request to document how to easily obtain the metadata needed for using lidar data read by laspy that is required to produce a georeferenced raster using GDAL.  The main challenge I have run into is getting the projection from the las file using laspy.  I've yet to figure out how to do this with 1.0 las files that I know have projection metadata. 

More generally, is there any interest in having a user forum to discuss usage of laspy where both laspy users and developers can ask questions not directly related to development of laspy, but geared at laspy usage?  Laspy is a library that sits in a wonderful ecosystem of open source geospatial software that is well supported by Python developers.  The laspy documentation does not explain how to use laspy in the broader context of other geospatial libraries -- such as GDAL -- that can be used to rasterize a point cloud.

issue_title: Improve build folder structure
issue_body:
Maybe the path should also reflect build type and version numbers?
The question is what should happen if no version is known or the build type is Default or wasn't specified...
- For unknown versions, instead of a folder like `v1.0.0` maybe `vAny`?
- For unspecified/default build type, maybe folder `Default`?

That aside, it would make sense to have paths such as `build/release/targetname/1.0.0/` (with subdirectories for external_sources, obj/source_subfolder, lib, bin, include and possibly others).

Furthermore, one could add a `package` flag to targets, which could be used to gather binaries and includes for a target and place them in corresponding folders, e.g. build/package/targetname/1.0.0/bin. Instead of a `package` flag, it could also be a build type (I would always want to create the package, but some may only want to do this when a release is published?).

So:
- build
  - release
    - targetname
      - 1.0.0
        - external_sources
        - obj
        - depfiles
        - lib
        - bin
        - include
      - 1.1.0
        - ...
  - debug
    - ...
  - package
    - targetname
      - 1.0.0
      - 1.1.0

issue_title: Output capture decorator
issue_body:
It's a little awkward making a callback function who's output should be captured:

```python
from ipywidgets import Output, IntSlider, VBox
from IPython.display import display
out = Output()
slider = IntSlider()
def change(change):
    with out:
        print('value: '+change.new)
slider.observe(change, 'value')
display(VBox([slider, out]))
```

Some people probably would prefer to have a decorator that automatically captures output:

```python
from ipywidgets import Output, IntSlider, VBox
from IPython.display import display
out = Output()
slider = IntSlider()

@out.capture() # new decorator - not sure if this is a good name for it...
def change(change):
    print('value: '+change.new)

slider.observe(change, 'value')
display(VBox([slider, out]))
```

issue_title: In some obscure cases, StopIteration exceptions can be turned into RuntimeErrors when passing through nurseries
issue_body:
There are some very obscure edge cases where using async generators does the wrong thing. For example, this code

```python
import trio

async def fail():
    raise ValueError

async def main():
    async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
        nursery.spawn(fail)
        raise StopIteration

trio.run(main)
```

Ought to raise a `MultiError([StopIteration(), ValueError()])`, but instead it raises a `MultiError([RuntimeError(), ValueError()])`. This is because you can't throw a `StopIteration` into an `async_generator`-based async generator (for details [see here](https://github.com/python-trio/trio/pull/228#pullrequestreview-44714634)) – it gets wrapped into a `RuntimeError`. If we let that `RuntimeError` propagate, then `acontextmanager` could turn it back, but here it gets wrapped into a `MultiError` instead so the magic doesn't work.

I guess we could make `acontextmanager` smart enough to look inside `MultiError`s? Or stop using `acontextmanager` and write this context manager directly? Or just not worry about it until we can drop 3.5 support and switch to using native async generators, which don't have this problem? It's *very* weird to have free-floating `StopIteration` exceptions wandering around like this. But I figured we should record the problem in any case.

issue_title: Ephemeral port exhaustion when accessing large amounts of stored objects
issue_body:
Recently I've been doing some work using this library in combination with django management commands to make exporting large amounts of data easy. For several days I was stuck with an error that seemed to be related to the number of calls I was making to minio to fetch stored images. After fetching 28,231 objects from minio, I was receiving this error:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/urllib3/connection.py", line 141, in _new_conn
    (self.host, self.port), self.timeout, **extra_kw)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/urllib3/util/connection.py", line 83, in create_connection
    raise err
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/urllib3/util/connection.py", line 73, in create_connection
    sock.connect(sa)
OSError: [Errno 99] Cannot assign requested address
```

 At first I believed this to be because the underlying sockets were getting stuck in TIME_WAIT, but had some difficulty testing that hypothesis. After some playing around with solutions, it seems to have actually been a garbage collection issue. Even though the library calls `self.file.close()` and `self.file.release_conn()` on the underlying file object, the reference to the HTTPConnection that is used to read from minio stays around and given enough requests are made, ties up the system so that no further requests can be made.

My solution to this was to manually set the filefields `file` property to `None`. I have created a custom storage that uses the `ReadOnlyMinioObjectFile`, but this issue seemed to be present on both classes. The solution I used:

```
# image is a model object, and data is a filefield on that object that uses minio as it's storage
with image.data.open('rb') as file_data:                                                                    
    data_bytes = file_data.read()                                                                 
    file_data.file = None  # Without this line, the process will fail after enough connections                                     
```

My use case is definitely an edge case and this problem probably wouldn't affect most users, but I thought it'd still be a good idea to put this out there in case anyone else ran into it.

issue_title: Optional attributes with no default values
issue_body:
Hi,

I  am trying to use the resnet 50 onnx model : https://github.com/onnx/models/tree/master/resnet50

And one of the convolution node doesn't have pads values.
If I look at the [doc of Conv](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#conv)
nothing informs the user that pads values are optional, and what are default values.

So, I took a look to the defs of Conv [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/c3137751923f576d14b4212ea444d1562b127a66/onnx/defs/nn/defs.cc#L170)

And to my surprise, all attributes are noticed as "OPTIONAL".
I also looked at schema.cc/h files, but nothing give me the answer.

Is it a bug in onnx to make all attributes as optional ? 
Or is it implicit in onnx that all attribute are optional and a default value is always provided to fill the attr.
In that case, what is the default value ?

In all cases, the doc is really unclear about this, some attr has the flag "(optional)" just after the name of the attribute like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-42)
for optional inputs it is in bold with the name like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#inputs-0---1)
and sometimes it is just into the description like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-55).
Moreover, it is the same with the flag "required" which is present just for few attr like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-55)

issue_title: Add ELMo (Deep contextualized word representations)
issue_body:
## Introduction
ELMo is a deep contextualized word representation that models both (1) complex characteristics of word use (e.g., syntax and semantics), and (2) how these uses vary across linguistic contexts (i.e., to model polysemy). These word vectors are learned functions of the internal states of a deep bidirectional language model (biLM), which is pre-trained on a large text corpus. They can be easily added to existing models and significantly improve the state of the art across a broad range of challenging NLP problems, including question answering, textual entailment and sentiment analysis.

### Other Tools

- [ ] [allenai](https://github.com/allenai/bilm-tf)
- [ ] [elmo](https://allennlp.org/elmo)

### Issue Description

It's not easy to use them.
I need a more simple and convenient tool like "Word2Vec" in "gensim"

issue_title: Notation3 parser dot issue
issue_body:
fertap...@gmail.com, 2011-03-24T17:17:56.000Z 
Hi,
I'm using FuXi as inference engine, and it receives the rules expressed in N3 format. Everything goes ok until I need to put a Freebase predicate such as http://rdf.freebase.com/ns/object.type.name.
The N3 parser doesn't distinguish between the "AND operator" dot and the dots of the predicate's URI.

This is an example of a dot used as AND operator:
{?P @has rdfs:range ?C. ?S ?P ?O} => {?O a ?C}.

and this is my N3 rule:
{?S fb:object.type.name ?O} => {?S a fb:namedPerson}.


and I get the error:
File "/Library/Python/2.6/site-packages/rdflib/plugins/parsers/notation3.py", line 1198, in path
    "EOF found in middle of path syntax")
rdflib.plugins.parsers.notation3.BadSyntax: at line 44 of <>:
Bad syntax (EOF found in middle of path syntax) at ^ in:
"@prefix log: .
@prefix str: .
@prefix owl: .
@prefix xsd: .
@prefix rdfs: .
@prefix rdf: .
@prefix e: .
@prefix fb: .
@prefix cc: .
@prefix : .


### Resource Description Framework RDF(S)

rdf:Alt rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:Container.
rdf:Bag rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:Container.
rdfs:ContainerMembershipProperty rdfs:subClassOf rdf:Property.
rdfs:Datatype rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:Class.
rdf:Seq rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:Container.
rdf:XMLLiteral rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:Literal; a rdfs:Datatype.

rdfs:comment rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Literal.
rdfs:domain rdfs:domain rdf:Property; rdfs:range rdfs:Class.
rdf:first rdfs:domain rdf:List; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource; a owl:FunctionalProperty.
rdfs:isDefinedBy rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource; rdfs:subPropertyOf rdfs:seeAlso.
rdfs:label rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Literal.
rdfs:member rdfs:domain rdfs:Container; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource.
rdf:object rdfs:domain rdf:Statement; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource.
rdf:predicate rdfs:domain rdf:Statement; rdfs:range rdf:Property.
rdfs:range rdfs:domain rdf:Property; rdfs:range rdfs:Class.
rdf:rest rdfs:domain rdf:List; rdfs:range rdf:List; a owl:FunctionalProperty.
rdfs:seeAlso rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource.
rdfs:subClassOf rdfs:domain rdfs:Class; rdfs:range rdfs:Class.
rdfs:subPropertyOf rdfs:domain rdf:Property; rdfs:range rdf:Property.
rdf:subject rdfs:domain rdf:Statement; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource.
rdf:type rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Class.
rdf:value rdfs:domain rdfs:Resource; rdfs:range rdfs:Resource.

rdf:nil a rdf:List.


### inference rules for RDF(S)

{?S fb:object.type.name ?O} => {?S a fb:conNombre}^..."

Thanks in advance

Comment 1 by gromgull
The rdflib behaviour appears to be right:

"{?S fb:object.type.name ?O} => {?S a fb:namedPerson}."

 is not valid n3, qnames cannot contain "." - look here: 

http://www.w3.org/2000/10/swap/grammar/n3-report.html#qname

There is no "." in the part of the regex after ":"  

The solution would be to redefine the prefix and qname, i.e. instead of: 

@prefix fb : 
fb:object.type.name

you must have:

@prefix fb : 
fb:name


Comment 2 by fertap...@gmail.com
Hi gromgull,
it worked. I tried to redefine the namespace earlier, but I had a typo error: instead of object.type.name is type.object.name, and I directly thought that it was a N3 parser error...what a stupid mistake.
Either way, thanks for your quick response.
Regards


Comment 3 by gromgull

issue_title: fix for GRAPH <some ctx> { ... }
issue_body:
drewpca, 2008-06-20T06:09:33.000Z 
A query like 
"SELECT ?s WHERE { GRAPH  { ?s ?p ?o }}"
would fail in the svn version of rdflib. Below is a patch with a test case
that was failing, plus a simple fix to sparql/Algebra.py that seems to work.



Index: test/test_sparql_graph_graph_pattern.py
===================================================================
--- test/test_sparql_graph_graph_pattern.py	(revision 1460)
+++ test/test_sparql_graph_graph_pattern.py	(working copy)
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
-# -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-
+# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
 from rdflib import ConjunctiveGraph, URIRef, Literal, RDFS
+from rdflib.Graph import Graph
 from StringIO import StringIO
 import unittest
 
@@ -37,5 +38,20 @@
             results == [doc1],
             "expecting : %s .  Got: %s"%([doc1],repr(results)))
 
+class TestSparqlGraph(unittest.TestCase):
+    """GRAPH <...> {...} blocks in a sparql query"""
+    def testGraph(self):
+        node = URIRef('http://example.com')
+        ctx = URIRef("http://example.com/other#context")
+        graph = ConjunctiveGraph()
+        
+        subgraph = Graph(store=graph.store, identifier=ctx)
+        subgraph.add((node, node, node))
+
+        allQuads = list(graph.quads((None, None, None)))
+        self.assertEqual(allQuads, [(node, node, node, subgraph)])
+        q = "SELECT ?s WHERE { GRAPH  {
?s ?p ?o }}"
+        self.assertEqual(len(list(graph.query(q))), 1)
+
Index: rdflib/sparql/Algebra.py
===================================================================
--- rdflib/sparql/Algebra.py	(revision 1460)
+++ rdflib/sparql/Algebra.py	(working copy)
@@ -1002,7 +1002,7 @@
                 #assert len(targetGraph) == 1
                 targetGraph = targetGraph[0]
             else:
-                targetGraph = Graph(tripleStore.store,graphName)
+                targetGraph = Graph(tripleStore.graph.store,graphName)
             tripleStore = sparqlGraph.SPARQLGraph(targetGraph,
                                                   dSCompliance=\
                                                   DAWG_DATASET_COMPLIANCE)
